Pak Study MCQs · pages 301-350

Sharpen your Pak Study recall

Tap a card to reveal the explanation. Take it one seal at a time.

500 questions in this set
1

The All India Muslim League rejected the Wavell Plan of 1945 for?

Correct Answer: It did not give League the sole right to nominate the Muslim Members in the Executive Council
Show explanation
Explanation

The All India Muslim League rejected the Wavell Plan of 1945

Because it did not grant the League exclusive rights to nominate all Muslim members to the Executive Council


2

Under Gandhi-Irwin pact of March 1931, it was decided that?

Correct Answer: The Congress will discontinue its Civil
Show explanation
Explanation

Gandhi-Irwin Pact, agreement signed on March 5, 1931

Between Mohandas K. Gandhi, leader of the Indian nationalist movement

Lord Irwin (later Lord Halifax), British viceroy (1926–31) of India

Participants: Mahatma Gandhi, George Montagu 

Date: March 5, 1931

Location: India

3

Urdu was declared Official Language of Pakistan under which article of the constitution 1973?

Correct Answer: Article 251
Show explanation
Explanation

The 1973 Constitution recognizes Urdu as Pakistan's only national language.

The 1973 Constitution contained:

  •  280 articles
  •  07 schedules with Objective Resolution.
  • 12 chapters 

    Effective date: 14 August 1973


    Drafted by the Government of Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto


    The Constitution declared Pakistan an Islamic Republic and Islam as the state religion.


    ND5-5-2023

    4

    Maryam Nawaz Sharif became first woman chief minister of which province of Pakistan?

    Correct Answer: Punjab
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Maryam Nawaz Sharif became first woman & 19th  CM Punjab in Feb 2024

    Predecessor: Moshin Naqvi

    Party Name: PML-N

    Note: Maryam Nawaz is the first woman to become Chief Minister of any province of Pakistan.

    5

    Wakhan Corridor, a narrow strip of Afghanistan separates Pakistan from which country?

    Correct Answer: Tajikistan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Wakhan Corridor is a narrow strip of territory in Afghanistan, extending to China and separating Tajikistan from Pakistan and Kashmir.

     The corridor was formed in 1893 and created the Durand Line.

    The Wakhan Corridor is about 350 km long.

    6

    What was the significances of 25th annual session of all India Muslim League?

    Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal Dilevered Allahabad Address
    Show explanation
    Explanation


    The significance of the 25th annual session of the All India Muslim League was Allama Iqbal's Allahabad Address

    On the afternoon of Monday, 29 December 1930, at Allahabad, India.

    7

    The Hindu’s had launched “Swadeshi Movement” (to boycott English-made goods) in the wake of?

    Correct Answer: Partition of Bengal 1905
    Show explanation
    Explanation

      It is in response to the partition of Bengal by the British.

      It was a campaign to boycott British goods and promote Indian industries.

      The Swadeshi Movement was launched on August 7, 1905,

      8

      The Constitution of All India Muslim League was drafted by a committee in 1907 headed by?

      Correct Answer: Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The constitution was framed in 1907 

      It is often associated with Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar

      He wrote the "Green Book," which served as its constitution. 

      He was among the leading figures of the Khilafat Movement. 

      *

      The first session of the party was held in Karachi in 1907.

      Muhammad Ali Jinnah joined the league in 1913.

      9

      Khawaja Nazim-ud-Din constituted the basic principles committee in 1949 for the purpose of _____?

      Correct Answer: To expedite the work of the Constitution making
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       The resolution proclaimed that the future constitution of Pakistan would not be modeled entirely on a European pattern but on the ideology and democratic faith of Islam.

      The resolution, in its entirety, has been made part of the Constitution of Pakistan under Article 2(A).

      The Basic Principles Committee was established on 12th March 1949 by Khawaja Nazimuddin on the instruction of Prime Minister Liaquat Ali Khan.

      10

      In Jhansi, the Muslim revolutionaries were led by _____?

      Correct Answer: Rani Lakshmi Bai
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Rani Lakshmi Bai was the central figure of the revolt in Jhansi, and both Hindus and Muslims fought under her leadership.

      She is remembered as a symbol of unity and resistance during the War of Independence 1857.

      11

      The step which ignited the War of Independence was the ______?

      Correct Answer: Introduction of greased cartridges
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In 1857, Indian soldiers (sepoys) were issued cartridges rumored to be greased with cow and pig fat.

      This hurt both Hindu and Muslim religious sentiments and triggered the War of Independence 1857.

      12

      Who is the current chairman of Gwadar Port Authority?

      Correct Answer: Mr. Noor-ul-Haq Baloch
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The current Chairman of the Gwadar Port Authority (GPA) is Mr. Noor-ul-Haq Baloch

      23 June 2025 till to date

      Click here for reference

      Updated & verified on 20 Dec 2025

       

      13

      Pakistan provides transit facilities to Afghanistan and landlocked countries of ______?

      Correct Answer: Central Asia
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan offers transit routes to Afghanistan and other landlocked Central Asian countries for trade and access to seaports.

      This facilitates regional connectivity and economic cooperation in Central Asia.

      14

      Simon commission was introduced in 1919 and decided that to send a commission to India after every _____ years.

      Correct Answer: 10
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • The Simon Commission was a result of the Government of India Act 1919,
      • Proposed sending a commission every 10 years.
      • It was actually sent in 1927, two years earlier than scheduled.
      • The Indian Statutory Commission, also known as the Simon Commission,
      • It was a group of seven members of the British Parliament,
      • Chaired by John Simon.
      15

      موئن جو دڑو کی دریافت کیسے ہوئی تھی؟

      Correct Answer: انسانی اعضاء کی ہڈیوں سے
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      موئن جو دڑو کی دریافت انسانی اعضاء کی ہڈیوں کی کھدائی کے دوران ہوئی تھی۔

      اس سے قدیم وادی سندھ کی تہذیب کے آثار ملے تھے۔

      16

      Nasir-ud-Din Muhammad Humayun was the _____ of Babur.

      Correct Answer: Elder son
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Humayun was the eldest son of Zahir-ud-Din Muhammad Babur, the founder of the Mughal Empire.

      He succeeded Babur as the second Mughal emperor in 1530.

      17

      گلہ بانی کا پیشہ کہاں عام ہے؟

      Correct Answer: بلوچستان
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      بلوچستان میں گلہ بانی کا پیشہ بہت عام ہے کیونکہ یہاں کی زیادہ آبادی دیہی اور چراگاہوں پر منحصر ہے۔

      اس خطے میں جانوروں کی دیکھ بھال اور مال مویشی پالنا روزمرہ کا اہم ذریعہ معاش ہے۔

      18

      Ranjit Singh originated from ______?

      Correct Answer: Punjab
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Maharaja Ranjit Singh was born in the Punjab region in 1780.

      He founded the Sikh Empire, which was based primarily in Punjab.

      19

      Swadeshi movement was launched to reverse the _____?

      Correct Answer: Partition of Bengal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      After the British Government's decision for the partition of Bengal 1903, the Swadeshi movement was formally started from Calcutta in 1905 to curb foreign goods by relying on domestic production.

       

      20

      فصلوں کی آب پاشی کے لیے کتنے طریقے ہیں؟

      Correct Answer: چار طریقے
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      فصلوں کی آب پاشی کے چار بنیادی طریقے ہوتے ہیں: سطحی، بارانی، چھڑکاؤ، اور قطرہ قطرہ۔

      ہر طریقہ زمین، فصل اور پانی کی دستیابی کے لحاظ سے استعمال ہوتا ہے۔

      21

      Benazir Bhutto’s second term in office was from _____?

      Correct Answer: 1993-1996
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Prime Minister

      She was sworn in as the Prime Minister of Pakistan on 2 December 1988.

      As Prime Minister first time from 1988 to 1990 

       As Prime Minister second time from 1993 to 1996.

      Farooq Laghari dissolved Benazir Bhutto's second government.

      She was the 11th and 13th Prime Minister of Pakistan.

      "All about Benazir Bhutto and important question"

      BB was Born on June 21, 1953, in Karachi

      BB was Assassinated on December 27, 2007, in Rawalpindi

      BB Spouse: Asif Ali Zardari (1987–2007)

      BB Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP)

      BB Education: Lady Margaret Hall (1973–1977),

      BB Children: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Bakhtawar Bhutto Zardari, Aseefa Bhutto Zardari.

      education :

      Which Nursery School did Benazir Bhutto attend? Lady Jennings Nursery School in Karachi.

      Which college did Benazir Bhutto attend? Radcliffe College

      Which University did Benazir Bhutto attend? University of Oxford

      22

      اسکائوٹ تنظیم میں شامل لڑکے کیا کہلاتے ہیں؟

      Correct Answer: بوائے اسکاؤٹس
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      اسکاؤٹ تنظیم میں شامل لڑکوں کو بوائے اسکاؤٹس کہا جاتا ہے۔

      یہ تنظیم لڑکوں کی تربیت، نظم و ضبط، خدمت اور کردار سازی کے لیے بنائی گئی تھی۔

      23

      اسکائوٹ تنظیم کے قیام کا مقصد کیا تھا؟

      Correct Answer: نوجوان کو مضبوط بنانا
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      اسکاؤٹ تنظیم کا مقصد نوجوانوں کی کردار سازی، نظم و ضبط اور خود اعتمادی پیدا کرنا تھا۔

      یہ تنظیم نوجوانوں کو جسمانی، اخلاقی اور معاشرتی طور پر مضبوط اور کارآمد شہری بناتی ہے۔

      24

      The real name of Muhammad bin Qasim was _____?

      Correct Answer: Amad-ud-din Muhammad
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Muhammad bin Qasim’s real name was Amad-ud-din Muhammad.

      He was a famous Arab general who led the conquest of Sindh in 712 AD.

      25

      There are ______ pillars of the Government in Pakistan.

      Correct Answer: Three
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Three Primary Branches of Government:
      Legislative:
      Power lies with the bicameral Parliament, consisting of:
      • National Assembly (Lower House)
      • Senate (Upper House)
      Executive:
      • Headed by the President of Pakistan
      • Actual executive powers are exercised by the Prime Minister and the Cabinet
      Judiciary:
      • Independent branch with authority vested in the Supreme Court
      • Ensures the interpretation and enforcement of the Constitution
      26

      What is the Fasting Buddha statue made from?

      Correct Answer: Stone
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Fasting Buddha statue is carved from stone, specifically black schist.

      It is a renowned Gandhara art piece, reflecting fine craftsmanship and spiritual symbolism.

      27

      Approximately how many pieces of the Indus Valley seals have been found?

      Correct Answer: 3000
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Archaeologists have discovered approximately 3000 Indus Valley seals at various sites.

      These seals often feature animals and script.

      They were likely used for trade and identification.

      28

      What was Sheikh Alam Din Ansari's profession?

      Correct Answer: Royal Physician
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sheikh Alam Din Ansari served as a Royal Physician during the Mughal period.

      He was known for his medical expertise and service in the royal court.

      29

      During whose reign was the Masjid Wazir Khan completed?

      Correct Answer: Shah Jahan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Wazir Khan Mosque is a 17th-century mosque located in the city of Lahore.

      The mosque was commissioned during the reign of the Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan.

      Opened: 1642

      30

      How old is the Great Bath of Mohenjo-Daro?

      Correct Answer: 4300 years
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Great Bath of Mohenjo-Daro dates back to around 2600 BCE, making it about 4300 years old.

      It is one of the earliest known public water tanks in the world.

      It is showing advanced urban planning of the Indus Valley Civilization.

      31

      Siddhartha was the original name of _____?

      Correct Answer: Buddha
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Buddha was a philosopher, mendicant, meditator, spiritual teacher, and religious leader who lived in ancient India.

      He is revered as the founder of the world religion of Buddhism, and worshipped by most Buddhist schools as the Enlightened One who has transcended Karma and escaped the cycle of birth and rebirth.

       

      Buddha Born: Lumbini, Nepal

      Buddha Died: Kushinagar, India

      Buddha Full name: Siddhartha Gautama

      32

      The announcement made on 16th June 1946 was in consultation with _____?

      Correct Answer: Cabinet Mission
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Cabinet Mission was sent to India in February 1946.

      They arrived in India on March 24, 1946.

      The Cabinet Mission Plan was announced on May 16, 1946.

      The plan was created by the Cabinet Mission, which was sent to India by British Prime Minister Clement Attlee to discuss transferring power from the British to the Indians.

      The mission's members (3) were Lord Pethick-Lawrence, Sir Stafford Cripps, and A. V. Alexander. 

      33

      Approximately how many people attended the All India Muslim League session in Lahore in 1940?

      Correct Answer: Over 1 lakh/More than one lakh
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The 1940 Lahore session of the All India Muslim League was a pivotal moment in the history of Pakistan's creation.

      It was during this session that the Lahore Resolution, also known as the Pakistan Resolution, was passed, calling for separate Muslim states.

      It is estimated that over 100,000 people attended this historic session, marking a significant turning point in the political landscape of British India.

      34

      In which year did a major shift occur in Sir Syed Ahmad Khan's thinking regarding Hindu-Muslim relations?

      Correct Answer: 1867
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In 1867, the Hindi-Urdu controversy deeply affected Sir Syed Ahmad Khan, changing his view of Hindu-Muslim unity.

      He realized that Muslims needed a separate political identity and educational progress.

      35

      What major development in Pakistan is linked to the arrival of Afghan refugees?

      Correct Answer: Origin of NGOs
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The arrival of Afghan refugees in the 1980s led to a humanitarian crisis in Pakistan.

      This prompted the growth of NGOs (Non-Governmental Organizations) to help with relief, shelter, education, and health.

      36

      When was the Constitution of the Islamic Republic of Pakistan enforced?

      Correct Answer: August 14, 1973
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Constitution of Pakistan (1973) was passed on April 10, 1973 and came into force on August 14, 1973.

      It established Pakistan as a parliamentary republic with Islamic provisions.

      37

      Makli Necropolis was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in ______?

      Correct Answer: 1981
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Makli Necropolis was inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site in 1981.

      Makli Necropolis, located in Thatta, Pakistan.

      It is one of the largest funerary sites in the world.

      It is the final resting place of some 125,000 local rulers, Sufi saints, and other eminent persons from the 14th to the 18th century.

      38

      Lahore resolution was seconded from Punjab by ______?

      Correct Answer: Maulana Zafar Ali Khan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Lahore Resolution is commonly known as the Pakistan Resolution.

      It was moved in the general session by A. K. Fazlul Huq, the Chief Minister of undivided Bengal.

      It was seconded by:

      • Chaudhry Khaliquzzaman from the United Provinces
      • Zafar Ali Khan from Punjab
      • Sardar Aurangzeb Khan from the North-West Frontier Province
      • Sir Abdullah Haroon from Sindh
      39

      How many representatives were given to Congress in the interim government?

      Correct Answer: 6
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The five ministers from the Muslim League for the interim Government were:

      • Liaquat Ali Khan.
      • Abdur Rab Nishtar.
      • Ibrahim Ismail Chundrigar.
      • Raja Ghazanfar Ali Khan.
      • Jogendra Nath Mandal.

      These members were joined at the request of Lord Wavell.

      Jawaharlal Nehru was the Prime Minister 

      Liaqat Ali Khan was assigned the portfolio of Finance

      *


      Additional Information:

      Lord Wavell wrote letters to Nehru and Jinnah on July 22, 1946, and invited them to join an “Interim Coalition Government.”

      He suggested that there would be 14 members in the cabinet:

      • 6 of them from Congress,
      • 5 from the Muslim League,
      • 3 would represent the minority parties
      40

      Who among the following was from the Punjab and supported the Lahore Resolution?

      Correct Answer: Dr. Muhammad Alam
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Dr. Muhammad Alam, a prominent leader from Punjab, actively supported the Lahore Resolution of 1940.

      The resolution was presented by A. K. Fazlul Haq of Bengal.


      Other notable supporters included:

      Chaudhry Khaliquzzaman from United Provinces

      Zafar Ali Khan from Punjab

      Sardar Aurangzeb Khan from North-West Frontier Province

      41

      What movement led to the change in Sir Syed's ideas in 1867?

      Correct Answer: Hindu opposition to Urdu in Banaras
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sir Syed is considered as the first person to theorize the idea of separate nationhood for Muslims in subcontinent.

      In a speech at Meerut in 1866 he presented on overall scenario of post colonial phase in

      which he described Muslims and Hindus as two nations

      Syed Ahmad Khan who presented the theory of the two nations 

      He presented this theory In a speech at Meerut in 1866 

      The two-nation theory is the basis of the creation of Pakistan.


      The theory advocated by the All India Muslim League is the founding principle of the Pakistan Movement.

      The state of India officially rejected the two-nation theory


      Aga Khan

      He called on the British Raj to consider Muslims to be a separate nation from India,

      the so-called 'Two Nation Theory"

      Abdul

      Ghaffar

      khan

      He founded the Khudai Khidmatgar commonly known as the RED SHIRTS,

      during the 1920s

      Awarded Bharat Ratna in 1987

      Abbas 

      Tyabji

      He is also known as the Grand Old Man of Gujarat

      Served as the Chief Justice of Baroda State

      Tyabji launched the Dharasana Satyagraha. in 1930



      42

      Which house of Parliament ensures provincial equality in representation?

      Correct Answer: Senate
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Senate of Pakistan ensures equal representation for all provinces, regardless of population size.

      This system promotes federal balance and protects provincial interests in legislation.


      After 18th Amendment  Senate consisted of 104 members 23 members  from each  province 

      After 2024 election now Senate members are 96

      Details of Seats of Senate of Pakistan

      Province/Terrotory

      General

      Technocrat/Ulema
