      96

      43

      Al-Shifa is the famous book of which one of the following scientist?

      Correct Answer: Bu-Ali-Sina
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      'Kitab Ash Shifa' written by Abu Ali ibn Sīna

      He began to compose the book in 1014

      Completed it around 1020,

      And published it in 1027.

      This book is also known as The Book of Healing.

      44

      To resolve the differences between East and West Pakistan regarding the ______, Prime Minister Muhammad Ali Bogra presented "Bogra Formula".

      Correct Answer: Constitution
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Prime Minister Muhammad Ali Bogra presented the Bogra Formula in 1953 to resolve differences between East and West Pakistan over constitutional representation, particularly in the national legislature.

      It aimed to provide parity between the two wings of the country.

      45

      Through the ______ of 1946, the Pakistan Resolution was amended.

      Correct Answer: Delhi resolution
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The resolution passed on April 19, 1946, at the Delhi convention, clarified doubts about the word 'states' in the original Lahore Resolution

      It demanded `an unequivocal understanding be given to implementing the establishment of Pakistan without delay’.

      NA23-7-2023



      46

      Molvi Tameez-ud-Deen was elected ______ of first constituent assembly of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Vice President
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Tameez Ud Deen was the Speaker and president of Pakistan's Constituent Assembly

      ***


      Maulvi Tamizuddin Khan was the Speaker of Pakistan's Constituent Assembly from 1948 to 1954

      and the National Assembly of Pakistan between 1962 and 1963.

      1st Deputy Speaker of the National Assembly of Pakistan from 23 February 1948 – 13 December 1948

      ****
      Born March 1889 Faridpur, Bengal Presidency, British India

      Died           19 August 1963 (aged 74) Dacca, East Pakistan, Pakistan

      Khan joined the non-cooperation movement led by Gandhi when he was a student.

      Later he joined the Indian National Congress and subsequently joined the khilafat movement in 1921

      ***

      NA23-7-2023


      47

      Through the Radcliffe Award, the Muslim-majority District of _____ was given to India.

      Correct Answer: Ferozpur
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Ferozpur was a Muslim-majority district that, despite expectations of going to Pakistan.

      It was awarded to India under the Radcliffe Award during the 1947 partition.

      This decision caused significant disappointment among Muslim leaders.

      It became a controversial point in partition history.

      48

      Pakistan's first ever Chinese language weekly newspaper is _____?

      Correct Answer: Huashang
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Huashang was launched as Pakistan's first Chinese-language weekly newspaper.

      It aims to enhance cultural and business ties between China and Pakistan, especially under CPEC.

      49

      Pakistan is one of the few countries in the world to have every of _____?

      Correct Answer: Geological Structures
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan features diverse geological structures such as mountains, plateaus, plains, deserts, and coastal areas.

      This geological diversity makes it unique and scientifically significant.

      50

      In 1971, Sheikh Mujeeb-ur-Rehman launched the _____?

      Correct Answer: Non-Cooperation Movement
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In March 1971, Sheikh Mujeeb-ur-Rehman initiated the Non-Cooperation Movement against the central government of Pakistan.

      It was a response to the denial of power transfer after the 1970 general elections.

      51

      Subtropical Continental Plateau includes western part of ______?

      Correct Answer: Balochistan
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Subtropical Continental Plateau lies in the western part of Balochistan, Pakistan.

      It features dry climate, rugged terrain, and sparse vegetation.

      52

      Which area of the glacier is controlled by Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Glacial valley and gyong La pass
      Show explanation
      Explanation


       کچھ ذرائع کے مطابق یہ لمبائی 72.4 کلومیٹر یعنی 45 میل ہے۔

      پیپر میں76 یا 72 آئے ان دونوں میں جو بھی آئے اس کے مطابق جواب دیں

      Siachen Glacier length is 76KM

      the height of Siachen Glacier is 20,000 feet

      Siachen Glacier is Pakistan's largest Glacier

      Siachen Glacier is the world 2nd largest Glacier

      “Siachen Glacier” is situated in Karakorum Range

      Siachen Glacier is located in Ghanche District, Gilgit-Baltistan. 

      meaning of Siachen Glacier is Abundance of roses.گلاب کی کثرت۔


      The Siachen conflict was a military conflict between India and Pakistan over the Siachen Glacier region in Kashmir.

      Started: 1984

      Ended: 2003

      53

      What was the number of Sugar mills in Pakistan in 1947?

      Correct Answer: 2
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The approximate number of sugar mills in Pakistan, as per PSMA (Pakistan Sugar Mills Association) is around 90,

      with 45 mills in Punjab and 45 in Sindh.

      Other sources mention 87 or 81 sugar mills operating in Pakistan. 

      54

      When did Musharraf took oath as President of Pakistan for his second term?

      Correct Answer: 16 Nov 2002
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Born: Delhi (August 11, 1943)

      Graduated: Pakistan Military Academy at Kakul (1964)

      Appointed chief of army staff: October 1998

      Appointer: Nawaz Sharif

      Musharraf became 10th president of Pakistan in 2001 until 2008.

      Coup: October 12, 1999 (declared a state of emergency)

      In June 2001, Musharraf became the president of Pakistan.

      In 2006, Musharraf’s autobiography titled In the Line of Fire was published.

      In March 2007, Musharraf suspended then chief justice Iftikhar Muhammad Chaudhry.

      Musharraf imposed a state of emergency on November 3, 2007.

      Resigned from his position of army chief November 2007

      Replacer: General Ashfaq Pervaiz Kayani

      Musharraf lifted the emergency on December 15, 2007.

      In 2010, Musharraf launched his political party — All Pakistan Muslim League (APML).

      He flew to Dubai on March 17, 2016

      On December 17, 2019, a special court handed Musharraf death sentence in the high treason case (only military ruler to be sentenced to death)

      Died: 5 February 2023

      Dying place: Dubai, UAE

      Disease: amyloidosis

      Aged: 79

      Buried: Karachi

      55

      Which of the following books was written by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan?

      Correct Answer: Tabbyin-Ul-Kalam
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Title: Tabyin-ul-Kalam

      Author: Sir Syed Ahmed Khan

      Language: Urdu

      Written around: 1862

      Purpose:

      • To compare Islam and Christianity
      • To promote religious harmony
      • To show similarities between the Bible and Islamic teachings
      56

      Which organization's rule ended in 1858 after Wood's Despatch?

      Correct Answer: East India Company
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      After the 1857 War of Independence, the British Government took direct control from the East India Company in 1858.

      Wood's Despatch (1854) laid the foundation for modern education just before the Company's rule ended.

      57

      After the failed war of 1857, who took over control of Indian affairs?

      Correct Answer: British Government
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      After the failure of the 1857 War of Independence, the British government ended the rule of the East India Company.

      India then came directly under the British Crown’s control, starting the period known as the British Raj.

      58

      Maulana Mufti Mahmood (RA) raised his voice against whose unconstitutional actions?

      Correct Answer: Ayub Khan and Iskander Mirza
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Maulana Mufti Mahmood opposed the unconstitutional actions and military interventions by Iskander Mirza and Ayub Khan.

      He was a strong voice for democracy and constitutional rule in Pakistan during their regimes.

      59

      Who was the Afghan ruler that fought in the Third Battle of Panipat?

      Correct Answer: Ahmed Shah Abdali
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Third Battle of Panipat took place on 14 January 1761

      This battle was fought at Panipat Delhi India

      It was fought between the:

      •  Maratha Empire (led by Sadashivrao Bhau)
      • Afghan army (of Ahmad Shah Durrani)

      Result: Durrani victory


      Additional information:

      1st battle of Panipat: 1526

      2nd battle of Panipat: 1556

      60

      Which of the following did not rule the Indian subcontinent?

      Correct Answer: Romans
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The British, Arabs, and Turks all established rule or influence in parts of the Indian subcontinent.

      The Romans never ruled the Indian subcontinent; they had trade contacts but no political control.

      61

      How many fighter planes crashed by Pakistani pilot in war of 1971 or 1965?

      Correct Answer: 5
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In the 1965 Indo-Pakistani war, Pakistani fighter pilot M.M. Alam shot down five Indian fighter planes within a minute. 

      Date: August – 23 September 1965

      Location: South Asia

      Result: Tashkent Declaration

      62

      When did the British Prime Minister declare to transfer power to Indians?

      Correct Answer: February 1947
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      On February 20, 1947, Attlee announced that British rule in India would end no later than June 1948.

      This declaration set the timeline for India's independence.

      63

      Which of these minerals is used in energy production and found in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Coal
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Coal is a major source of energy.

      It is used in power generation.

      Pakistan has significant coal reserves, especially in the Thar region of Sindh.

      64

      Which highway is a symbol of Pakistan-China economic cooperation?

      Correct Answer: Karakoram Highway
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Karakoram Highway is a key route in CPEC.

      It connecting Pakistan with China through the Khunjerab Pass.

      It is often referred to as the “Eighth Wonder of the World” due to its high-altitude engineering.

      65

      Which movements widened the gap between Hindus and Muslims?

      Correct Answer: Shuddhi and Sangathan
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      Shuddhi Movement (1920s):
      Led by Hindu reformist groups (e.g., Arya Samaj) to "reconvert" Muslims and Christians back to Hinduism.
      Muslims perceived it as an aggressive effort to erase their religious identity.
      Sangathan Movement:
      • A Hindu organizational response to consolidate Hindu unity, often framed as countering Muslim solidarity.
      • Deepened mutual suspicion and communal polarization.
      66

      Which organization is responsible for regulating universities in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Higher Education Commission (HEC)
      Show explanation
      Explanation

       In Pakistan, the main organization responsible for regulating universities is the Higher Education Commission (HEC)

      It was established in 2002 .

      First Chairmanship of Atta-ur-Rahman.

      Its main functions are funding, overseeing, regulating and accrediting the higher education institutions in the country.

      67

      The British Crown assumed direct control over India in which year?

      Correct Answer: 1858
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The British Crown assumed direct control over India in 1858, following the Indian Rebellion of 1857.

      It is also known as the Sepoy Mutiny.

      This marked the end of the East India Company's rule and the beginning of the British Raj, which lasted until India gained independence in 1947.

      The British ruled India directly for 90 years, from 1858 to 1947.

      68

      Who overthrew Nawaz Sharif's government in 1999?

      Correct Answer: General Pervez Musharraf
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In October 1999, General Pervez Musharraf led a military coup to overthrow Prime Minister Nawaz Sharif.

      The military seized control of the government on October 12,

      And by October 14, Musharraf was acting as Chief Executive.

      69

      Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental (MAO) College was established by ______?

      Correct Answer: Sir Syed Ahmed
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      • Sir Syed Ahmad Khan established the Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental (MAO) College
      • Foundation Date: May 24, 1875 (as a school).
      • Location: Aligarh, Uttar Pradesh.

      Additional information

      • Became a college two years later (1877)
      • Known initially as Madarsatul Uloom
      • Inspired by the Cambridge education system,
      • Began operations on Queen Victoria's 56th birthday
      • Intermediate classes were introduced in 1878.
      • B.A. degree classes in 1881
      • Aligarh Muslim University in 1920


      Last updated question by NA 22-April-2026

      70

      Who evaluated the success of Wood's Despatch?

      Correct Answer: Hunter Commission
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Hunter Commission (1882) was appointed to evaluate the outcomes of Wood’s Despatch (1854) on education in India.

      It recommended the promotion of primary and secondary education, especially through Indian languages.

      71

      The last Mughal King Bahadur Shah Zafar died in ______?

      Correct Answer: Rangoon
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Bahādur Shāh II, also called Bahādur Shāh Ẓafar, (born October 24, 1775, Delhi)

      Bahadur Shah Zafar is the last Mughal emperor of India (reigned 1837–57).

      Bahadur Shah Zafar died in the Rangoon jail in 1862

      Bahadur Shah Zafar was 83 years old when he arrived in Rangoon.

      He had developed paralysis in the throat region and finally succumbed to it on 7 November 1862.

      He was buried in an anonymous grave.

      After the War of Independence, Bahadur Shah Zafar was tried by the British deposed, and deported to:  Rangoon in British-controlled Burma in 1858

      ND23-4-2023

      72

      What major national movement did Maulana Mufti Mehmood (RA) joined in 1944?

      Correct Answer: Quit India Movement
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      In 1944, Maulana Mufti Mehmood (RA) joined the Quit India Movement, a significant campaign launched by the Indian National Congress to demand an end to British rule.
      He aligned with Jamiat Ulema-e-Hind, which supported a united India at that time.
      73

      What material were the earliest coins in the subcontinent made of?

      Correct Answer: Silver
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The earliest coins in the subcontinent, known as punch-marked coins, were primarily made of silver.

      These coins date back to around the 6th century BCE during ancient Indian kingdoms.

      74

      With whom did the Afghans confront in the Third Battle of Panipat?

      Correct Answer: Marathas
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Third Battle of Panipat took place on 14 January 1761

      This battle was fought at Panipat Delhi India

      It was fought between the:

      •  Maratha Empire (led by Sadashivrao Bhau)
      • Afghan army (of Ahmad Shah Durrani)

      Result: Durrani victory


      Additional information:

      1st battle of Panipat: 1526

      2nd battle of Panipat: 1556

      75

      Name the athlete who won the first and only gold medal in Asian Athletics Championship 2025 in South Korea?

      Correct Answer: Arshad Nadeem
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Olympic champion Arshad Nadeem threw a massive 86.40m to win gold in the men’s javelin final at the Asian Athletics Championship

      Winning date: 31 May 2025

      Venue: Gumi, South Korea

      Nadeem’s gold is Pakistan’s first and only medal at the championship.

       

      Additional Information:

      The 2025 Asian Athletics Championships was the 26th edition of the Asian Athletics Championships.

      Being held from 27 to 31 May 2025 in Gumi, South Korea.

       ****

      76

      Which Pakistani athlete won gold medal in Asian Athletics Championship 2025?

      Correct Answer: Arshad Nadeem
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Olympic champion Arshad Nadeem threw a massive 86.40m to win gold in the men’s javelin final at the Asian Athletics Championship

      Winning date: 31 May 2025

      Venue: Gumi, South Korea

      Nadeem’s gold is Pakistan’s first and only medal at the championship.

       

      Additional Information:

      The 2025 Asian Athletics Championships was the 26th edition of the Asian Athletics Championships.

      Being held from 27 to 31 May 2025 in Gumi, South Korea.

       ****

      77

      What is considered essential for a county's industrial development?

      Correct Answer: Raw material
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Raw materials are essential as they provide the basic inputs needed for industries to manufacture goods.

      Availability of raw materials reduces production costs and supports sustained industrial growth.

      78

      In which international organization do Pakistan and China often share the same stance?

      Correct Answer: UN
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan and China often align their views in the United Nations (UN) on issues like sovereignty, non-interference, and global peace.

      Both support each other’s positions on international matters such as Kashmir (Pakistan) and Taiwan (China) within the UN framework.

      79

      During World War I, which side did the Ottoman Caliphate support?

      Correct Answer: Germany
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      During World War I, the Ottoman Caliphate allied with the Central Powers, including Germany and Austria-Hungary.

      This alliance led to the eventual defeat and disintegration of the Ottoman Empire.

      80

      What type of government system is mentioned as being practiced in Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Democracy
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pakistan follows a democratic system, where representatives are elected by the people through voting.

      It is a parliamentary democracy, although its democratic practices have faced challenges over time.

      81

      What was established in 1875 as part of the Aligarh Movement?

      Correct Answer: Muhammadan Anglo Oriental School
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Muhammadan Anglo-Oriental (MAO) School was established by Sir Syed Ahmed Khan in 1875 as part of the Aligarh Movement.

      It later evolved into Aligarh Muslim University, aiming to modernize Muslim education.

      82

      Which language did Macaulay promote as key to success in his minute?

      Correct Answer: English
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      In his famous Minute on Education (1835), Thomas Babington Macaulay promoted English as the medium of instruction.

      He believed English was essential for creating a class of Indians who could assist in British administration.

      83

      What portion of his property did Quaid-e-Azam dedicate to Islamia College Peshawar?

      Correct Answer: One-third
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah donated one-third of his property to Islamia College Peshawar.

      This act reflected his strong support for education and the role of the college in Pakistan’s movement.

      84

      What title is given to Sir Syed Ahmed Khan due to his ideology?

      Correct Answer: Founder of Two Nation Theory
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Syed Ahmad Khan who presented the theory of the two nations 

      He presented this theory In a speech at Meerut in 1866 

      The two-nation theory is the basis of the creation of Pakistan.


      The theory advocated by the All India Muslim League is the founding principle of the Pakistan Movement.

      The state of India officially rejected the two-nation theory


      Aga Khan

      He called on the British Raj to consider Muslims to be a separate nation from India,

      the so-called 'Two Nation Theory"

      Abdul

      Ghaffar

      khan

      He founded the Khudai Khidmatgar commonly known as the RED SHIRTS,

      during the 1920s

      Awarded Bharat Ratna in 1987

      Abbas 

      Tyabji

      He is also known as the Grand Old Man of Gujarat

      Served as the Chief Justice of Baroda State

      Tyabji launched the Dharasana Satyagraha. in 1930


      85

      What was Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum Khan's first job in education?

      Correct Answer: School Teacher
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum Khan started his career as a school teacher.

      Sahibzada Abdul Qayyum Khan founded Islamia College in Peshawar, KPk.

      He founded the college in 1913, inspired by the visionary educational principles of Sir Syed Ahmad Khan

      In 1937, he became the first Chief Minister of Khyber-Pakhtunkhwa

      86

      Where is Sardar Nishtar buried?

      Correct Answer: In the vicinity of Mazar-e-Quaid, Karachi
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Sardar Abdur Rab Nishtar was born on June 13, 1899, in Peshawar, British India.

      He was died on February 14, 1958, in Karachi, Pakistan.

      87

      On ___ 1988 Benazir Bhutto become the first woman PM of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: 2nd December
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Prime Minister

      She was sworn in as the Prime Minister of Pakistan on 2 December 1988.

      As Prime Minister first time from 1988 to 1990 

       As Prime Minister second time from 1993 to 1996.

      Farooq Laghari dissolved Benazir Bhutto's second government.

      She was the 11th and 13th Prime Minister of Pakistan.

      "All about Benazir Bhutto and important question"

      BB was Born on June 21, 1953, in Karachi

      BB was Assassinated on December 27, 2007, in Rawalpindi

      BB Spouse: Asif Ali Zardari (1987–2007)

      BB Party: Pakistan Peoples Party (PPP)

      BB Education: Lady Margaret Hall (1973–1977),

      BB Children: Bilawal Bhutto Zardari, Bakhtawar Bhutto Zardari, Aseefa Bhutto Zardari.

      education :

      Which Nursery School did Benazir Bhutto attend? Lady Jennings Nursery School in Karachi.

      Which college did Benazir Bhutto attend? Radcliffe College

      Which University did Benazir Bhutto attend? University of Oxford,

      *******

      ND13-5-2023


      88

      In which of the following cities Indian National Congress was found?

      Correct Answer: Bombay
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Indian National Congress was founded at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay On 28 December 1885.

      Allan Octavian Hume Place assumed office as the General Secretary,

      Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee of Calcutta was elected first President.

      Founders: Allan Octavian Hume Place

      89

      When the movement of civil disobedience was against the British started in KPK?

      Correct Answer: April 1930
      Show explanation
      Explanation
      The Civil Disobedience Movement was formally launched by Mahatma Gandhi on 6 April 1930.
      It began with breaking the salt law after Gandhi’s historic Dandi March.
      In Khyber Pakhtunkhwa (Peshawar), it gained momentum with the Qissa Khwani Bazaar massacre on April 23, 1930.
      90

      Who became first Hindu woman Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP) of Pakistan?

      Correct Answer: Manisha Ropeta
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Manisha Ropeta became first Hindu woman Deputy Superintendent of Police (DSP) of Pakistan in July 2022.

      Manisha Ropeta belongs to Jacobobad, Sindh.


      91

      Allama Iqbal Open University was established under which policy?

      Correct Answer: 1972
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      The Open University (AIOU) was established in May 1974 under the Education Policy of 1972-80 to provide flexible learning opportunities.

      It was created by an act of Parliament to help those unable to attend full-time studies due to work or other commitments.

      92

      Which among the following is not the coastal line of Pakistan? Pasni , Ormara, Turbat , None of these

      Correct Answer: None of these
      Show explanation
      Explanation

      Pasni, Ormara, and Turbat: are all located on the coast of Pakistan, specifically in the Makran Coastal belt.

      Therefore, "None of these" is the correct answer

      • The new length of the Coastline of Pakistan is 1,058 km (650 miles).
        • The old length of the Coastline of Pakistan is 1,046 km (650 miles).
        • It is divided into two parts: the Sindh Coast, stretching 270 km, and the Makran Coast, extending 720 km.
        • The majority of Pakistan's coastline is located in Balochistan.
        • Geographically, the coast of Pakistan includes the Gwadar Coast, Lasbela Coast, Karachi Coast, Thatta Coast.
        • Thatta Coast from Korangi Creek to the Indian border at Sir Creek.
        • The Rann of Kutch from Badin to Tharparkar District.
        93

        Which is the biggest ship breaking company in Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Gadani
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Gadani ship-breaking yard is the world's third largest ship breaking yard located across a 10 km long beachfront at Gadani.

        Gadani is is located about 40 km northwest of Karachi.

        *** 

        94

        After independence, which was the first Organization Pakistan joined?

        Correct Answer: FAO
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        British Commonwealth Pakistan emerged as an independent state on 14th August 1947.

        Pakistan joined FAO on 7 September 1947.

        Pakistan joined UNESCO on 14 September 1949.

        Pakistan joined World Bank in 1950.

        Pakistan joined IMF on 11 July 1950.

        Pakistan joined SEATO in 1954.

        95

        Which is the second longest river of Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: Sutlej River
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        List of Longest Rivers of Pakistan
        1. Indus River:  3,180 km (1,980 miles).
        2. Sutlej River:  1,450 km (900 miles).
        3. Chenab River: 960 km (600 miles)
        4. Jhelum River: 725 km (450 miles).
        5. Ravi River: 720 km (450 miles).
        *********
        The largest river in Pakistan is the Indus River
        The Indus River starts in Tibet
        The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake
        The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,
        The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.
        River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta
        The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu
        National River of Pakistan: Indus River
        6 Barrages constructed in Indus River
        Indus River Length: 3,180 km
        Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo
        The upper Indus Plain ends at Mithankot 
        The upper Indus Plain starts from Kalabagh

        **********

        How many rivers flow in Punjab Province?

        5

        How many rivers flow in Sindh Province?

        4

        How many rivers flow in Balochistan Province?

        7

        How many rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province?

        8

        How many rivers are present in Pakistan?

        24

        96

        Which Pakistani scientist developed the first indigenous satellite, Badr-1?

        Correct Answer: Dr. Salim Mehmud
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Badr-1 was Pakistan's first indigenously built artificial satellite, launched on 16 July 1990.

        Dr. Salim Mehmud led the development team as the technical director.

        Dr. Muhammad Riaz Suddle served as the project manager.

        97

        How many persons with Pakistani origin have been awarded the Nobel Prize till to date?

        Correct Answer: 2
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Two persons of Pakistani origin have received the Nobel Prize: Abdus Salam (Physics, 1979) and Malala Yousafzai (Peace, 2014).

        Abdus Salam was recognized for his work in theoretical physics, while Malala was honored for her activism in girls' education.

        98

        The oldest airport of Pakistan is located in which city?

        Correct Answer: Lahore
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The oldest continuously operating airport in Pakistan is located in Lahore

        This airport was known as Walton Airport, which was founded in 1918.

        It is named after Sir Colonel Cusack Walton.

        The airport was active until 1962.

        It is now defunct for private usage as well.

        It is now a defunct airport and has been converted into the Central Business District of Punjab.


        Additional information 

        The airport is named after a former head of a flying school, namely Sir Colonel Cusack Walton.

        On 21st August 2021, the Government of Punjab. leased the land to LCBDDA (Lahore Central Business District Development Authority) 

        99

        CPEC is a part of which larger initiative?

        Correct Answer: Belt and Road Initiative (BRI)
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        CPEC (China-Pakistan Economic Corridor) is a major component of China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI),

        100

        CPEC (China-Pakistan Economic Corridor) starts from which city in China?

        Correct Answer: Kashgar
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        CPEC begins in Kashgar, a city in the Xinjiang region of China, and extends to Gwadar Port in Pakistan.

      • China Pakistan Economic Corridor is a framework for regional connectivity. CPEC  not only benefits China and Pakistan, but also has a positive impact on Iran, Afghanistan, the Central Asian Republic, and the region.
      • CEPAC Established in 22 May 2013 (9 years ago)
      • Budget China Development Bank
      • Asian Infrastructure Investment Bank
      • Silk Road Fund
        • 101

        Which airport was constructed under CPEC?

        Correct Answer: Gwadar International Airport
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Gawadar International Airport was constructed under the China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC).

        It is the largest airport in Pakistan by area

        Occupying 4,300 acres (17 km2) of land

        Opened 20 January 2025

        102

        What is the official motto of the Pakistan Army?

        Correct Answer: Faith, Piety, Struggle in the name of God
        Show explanation
        Explanation

         The official motto of the Pakistan Army is "Iman, Taqwa, Jihad fi-Sabilillah"

        Translates to "Faith, Piety, Struggle in the Name of God".

        This motto emphasizes the concept of "struggle" (Jihad) in the service of God. 

        Founded: 14 August 1947

        Colours white and green
        Anniversaries: Defence Day: 6 September
        103

        Who was the second wife of Quaid-e-Azam?

        Correct Answer: Ratan bai
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Emibai Jinnah was the first cousin and wife of Muhammad Ali Jinnah from 1892 until 1893.

        Rattanbai Jinnah was 2nd the wife of Muhammad Ali Jinnah from 1918 till 1929

        ****

        ***

        Mohammed Ali Jinnah died on September 11, 1948, in Karachi, Pakistan.

        He was the founder and first governor-general (1947–48) of Pakistan

        He Born: December 25, 1876, Karachi

        Quaid-e-Azam Residency also known as Ziarat Residency.

        It is located in Ziarat, Balochistan, Pakistan.

        104

        Pakistan is located in the ____ part of South Asia.

        Correct Answer: Western
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Pakistan is located in the western part of South Asia.

        It shares borders with India to the east, Afghanistan and Iran to the west, and China to the north.

        105

        Allama Iqbal was awarded the title of "Sir" by ______?

        Correct Answer: King George V
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • Allama Iqbal received the title of Sir in 1922 in Lahore.
        • He was knighted by King George V for his contributions to literature.

        Note: According to SPSC, 1923 is the correct answer.

        106

        ASF is a _____ department.

        Correct Answer: Federal
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        ASF (Airport Security Force) is a Federal department.

        It established under the ASF Act of 1975.

        It operates under the Secretary of Aviation.

        It is responsible for airport security, including protecting facilities and aircraft in Pakistan.

        107

        The Term Hijacking refer to ________?

        Correct Answer: Unlawful seizure of aircraft
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Hijacking generally refers to the illegal seizure of a land vehicle, ship, or aircraft in transit and its forcible diversion to a new destination against the will of its crew.

        ہائی جیکنگ سے مراد عام طور پر ٹرانزٹ میں زمینی گاڑی، جہاز یا ہوائی جہاز کا غیر قانونی قبضہ اور اس کے عملے کی مرضی کے خلاف اسے زبردستی نئی منزل کی طرف موڑ دینا ہے۔



        108

        On February 11th, 2021 Pakistan conducted a successful training launch of ____ cruise missile.

        Correct Answer: Babur
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        On 11 February 2021, Pakistan conducted a successful launch of the Babur-1A cruise missile with a range of 450 kilometers.

        while 

        Pakistan successfully conducted a flight test of air-launched cruise missile Ra’ad-II on 18th February 2020.

        Pakistan test-fired its first submarine-launched cruise missile Babur-III on 9 January 2017.

        109

        Who among the following hijacked passenger train in Balochistan in March 2025?

        Correct Answer: Balochistan Liberation Army (BLA)
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The train named Jaffar Express hijacked by BLA in Balochistan in March 2025.

        The train was targeted by terrorists in the area of Bolan, Balochistan on March 11, 2025.

        The banned Baloch Liberation Army (BLA) claimed responsibility for the attack.

         

        Additional Information:

        As per ISPR, all 33 terrorists killed by Security forces.

        21 passengers and 4 Frontier Corps personnel dead in this incident

        ***

        110

        What is the role of the Special Investment Facilitation Council (SIFC)?

        Correct Answer: To promote foreign and domestic investment in Pakistan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        SIFC: Special Investment Facilitation Council

        Formation: 17th June, 2023

        Founded by: Shahbaz Sharif

        Headquarter: Islamabad

        Purpose: Economic revival of Pakistan, attract Foreign Direct Investment

        111

        Sovereignty belongs to which one of the following?

        Correct Answer: Allah
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        In Islamic belief, sovereignty belongs to Allah alone.

        Allah is the supreme authority over all creation. 

        Human governance must align with divine laws from the Quran and Hadith.

        112

        Constitution(s) having preamble(s) based on Objective Resolution?

        Correct Answer: Both B and C
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The Constitutions having preambles based on the Objective Resolution are "1956" and "1973."

        The Objective Resolution, adopted in 1949, was incorporated into these Constitutions to reflect its principles.

        113

        How many airports are under ASF?

        Correct Answer: 42
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        42 airports are under ASF           

        ASF started its Journey by managing the security of 7 major airports with a limited manpower of 2870 of all ranks.

        Subsequently, it was mandated to provide security to all 42 airports in Pakistan with a manpower of 8945 personnel.

        ***

        Founded: 1976

        Abbreviation: ASF

        Parent agency: Cabinet Secretariat, Aviation Division, Pakistan


        ND-31-8--2023


        114

        Which article of Pakistan constitution talks about a clean environment?

        Correct Answer: Article 9A
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Article 9A guarantees the right to a clean, healthy, and sustainable environment.

        It was introduced through the 26th Amendment to the Constitution.

        It explicitly states: “Every person shall be entitled to a clean, healthy and sustainable environment.”

        115

        The first ruler of Kalhora dynasty in Sindh was ______?

        Correct Answer: Mian Yar Muhammad Kalhoro
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The first ruler of the Kalhora dynasty in Sindh was Mian Yar Muhammad Kalhoro. 

        Mian Yar Muhammad Kalhoro was granted the title of Khuda Yar Khan by the Mughals.

        He was established their rule in Sindh around the beginning of the 18th century.

        The last ruler of the Kalhora dynasty in Sindh was Mian Abdul Nabi Kalhoro.

        116

        What is the correct formula to calculate a winner’s election percentage?

        Correct Answer: (Winner’s votes ÷ Total votes) × 100
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The correct formula to calculate a winner's election percentage is:

        (Winner’s votes ÷ Total votes) × 10

        117

        The general elections were held in ____ provinces of India from February to March 1937.

        Correct Answer: 11
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Elections were held in eleven provinces - Madras, Central Provinces, Bihar, Orissa, United Provinces, Bombay Presidency, Assam, NWFP, Bengal, Punjab and Sindh.

        Indian National Congress won 8 provinces.

        118

        When first case of Omicron sub-variant detected in Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: May 9th 2022
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        National Institute of Health on 9th May 2022 said that the first case of the subvariant of COVID-19's Omicron variant has been confirmed in Pakistan.

        119

        Which is the first private Airport in Pakistan?

        Correct Answer: SialKot
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Sialkot International Airport is the first privately owned public airport in Pakistan.

        It was built by the business community of Sialkot by their own efforts.

        120

        When was the last general census in Pakistan conducted?

        Correct Answer: 2023
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        There have been only 7 nationwide censuses.

        1. First Census was held in Pakistan in 1951.
        2. The second Census was held in Pakistan in 1961.
        3. The third Census was held in Pakistan in 1972.
        4. The fourth Census was held in Pakistan in 1981.
        5. The fifth Census was held in Pakistan in 1998.
        6. The Sixth Census was held in Pakistan in 2017.
        7. The seventh census was held in Pakistan in 2023.


        Additional Information:

        Census has been undertaken every 10 years

        it was beginning in 1872 under British viceroy Lord Mayo

        agm.5.3.23na19112023

        121

        Who is the first woman woman from the Christian and Minority community to achieve the rank of Brigadier in Pakistan Army?

        Correct Answer: Helen Mary Roberts
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        In May 2024, Dr Helen Mary Roberts has been promoted to the rank of brigadier in the Pakistan Army Medical Corps.

        Helen Mary Roberts  is the the first female brigadier to hail from the country’s Christian community.

        ***


        122

        A proposal for the establishment of the All India Muslim League was presented by ______?

        Correct Answer: Sir Saleemullah Khan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        The proposal for the All India Muslim League was presented in 1906.

        It was during the Dhaka session of the All India Muhammadan Educational Conference.

        Sir Saleemullah Khan formally proposed it.

        Supported by:

        • Hakim Ajmal Khan
        • Maulana Muhammad Ali Jauhar
        • Zafar Ali Khan
        • Syed Nabiullah (barrister from Lucknow)
        • Ibraheem Fazili
        • Syed Zahur Ahmad (eminent lawyer)
        • And several others.
        123

        What was the amount of the IMF loan provided to Pakistan in June 2023?

        Correct Answer: $3 billion
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        In June 2023, the IMF approved a $3 billion loan to Pakistan under a 9-month Stand-By Arrangement (SBA).

        This loan aimed to stabilize Pakistan's economy, support its financial reforms, and help manage external debt obligations.

        124

        Mohmand dam is proposed to be constructed on _______ River?

        Correct Answer: Swat River
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Mohmand Dam

        Inaugurated by: Prime Minister Imran Khan on May 2, 2019

        Expected Completion: November 2024

        Type: Multi-purpose concrete-faced rock-filled dam

        Location: Swat River, Mohmand District, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan

        Construction Cost: ₨340 billion (US$1.2 billion)

        125

        The first international organization joined by Pakistan was the ______?

        Correct Answer: British CommonWealth
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        British Commonwealth Pakistan emerged as an independent state on 14th August 1947.

        Pakistan joined FAO on 7 September 1947.

        Pakistan joined UNESCO on 14 September 1949.

        Pakistan joined World Bank in 1950.

        Pakistan joined IMF on 11 July 1950.

        Pakistan joined SEATO in 1954.

        126

        General Muhammad Ayub Khan forced the powerless President ______ to resign.

        Correct Answer: Islander Mirza
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        General Ayub Khan forced President Iskander Mirza to resign on October 27, 1958, after imposing martial law on October 7, 1958.

        Mirza, Pakistan’s first President.

        He was exiled to London, where he died in 1969.

        127

        پاکستان میں دریاؤں کے کتنے نظام ہیں؟

        Correct Answer: تین
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Three main river systems:


        1. دریائے سندھ کا نظام (The Indus River System)  

        This vast river network originates in Tibet and flows through India and Pakistan, with major tributaries like the

        • Jhelum
        • Chenab
        • Ravi
        • Beas
        • Sutlej

        2. اندروی دریاؤں کا نظام (The Internal Rivers System)  

        In southwestern Balochistan, some rivers, instead of flowing into the sea, terminate in lakes.


        3. ساحلی علاقوں کے دریاؤں کا نظام (The Coastal Rivers System)  

         Flow directly into the Indian Ocean or the Arabian Sea

        128

        Upon arriving in Sind, Muhammad bin Qasim established and controlled the Indus Valley as far north as which city?

        Correct Answer: Multan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Muhammad bin Qasim controlled the Indus Valley as far north as the city of Multan.

        He defeated Raja Dahir, the last Hindu ruler of Sindh

        From 708 to 711, Muhammad ibn al-Qasim led the Sindh conquest

        129

        Which son of Raja Dahir embraced Islam?

        Correct Answer: Jai Singh
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Jai Singh, a son of Raja Dahir, did embrace Islam.

        He was later killed by Junaid bin Abdur Rahman. 


        Raja Dahir was the last Hindu ruler of Sindh in present-day Pakistan.

        In 712 CE his kingdom was invaded by the Umayyad Caliphate led by Muhammad bin Qasim

        where Dahir died while defending his kingdom.

        Aror was the capital of King Dahir

        ******

        راجہ داہر موجودہ پاکستان میں سندھ کے آخری ہندو حکمران تھے۔ 712عیسوی میں اس کی سلطنت پر اموی خلافت نے حملہ کیا جس کی قیادت محمد بن قاسم کر رہے تھے۔

        جہاں داہر اپنی سلطنت کا دفاع کرتے ہوئے مر گیا۔

        130

        What is the name of the operation under which Pakistan has carried out precision strikes to response Indian aggression in May 2025?

        Correct Answer: Operation Bunyan Um Marsoos
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Pakistan launched precision missile strikes against India under Operation Bunyan Um Marsoos.

        Bunyan Ul Marsoos is an Arabic verse from the Quran.

        The operation’s name—meaning “a structure firmly joined together”—draws inspiration from Surah Al-Saff (61-4), which praises those who fight in Allah’s cause as a solid, fortified structure.

        إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الَّذِينَ يُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِهِ صَفًّا كَأَنَّهُم بُنْيَانٌ مَّرْصُوصٌ

        Translation: ‘Allah indeed loves those who fight in His Way as though they are a solid wall cemented with molten lead’ (Surah Al-Saff, verse 4).

        This operation was launched on 10 May 2025 (after Fajr prayer)

        Seven sites in India were targeted, including the key Pathankot air base, Udhampur air base, and Gujarat air base. Rajasthan air base and Brahmos storage site.

        The operation was launched in retaliation for Indian missile strikes that hit several locations inside Pakistan.

         ****

        131

        What is the name of the military operation launched by Pakistani Army in May 2025 to response Indian aggression?

        Correct Answer: Operation Bunyan Um Marsoos
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Pakistan launched precision missile strikes against India under Operation Bunyan Um Marsoos.

        Bunyan Ul Marsoos is an Arabic verse from the Quran.

        The operation’s name—meaning “a structure firmly joined together”—draws inspiration from Surah Al-Saff (61-4), which praises those who fight in Allah’s cause as a solid, fortified structure.

        إِنَّ اللَّهَ يُحِبُّ الَّذِينَ يُقَاتِلُونَ فِي سَبِيلِهِ صَفًّا كَأَنَّهُم بُنْيَانٌ مَّرْصُوصٌ

        Translation: ‘Allah indeed loves those who fight in His Way as though they are a solid wall cemented with molten lead’ (Surah Al-Saff, verse 4).

        This operation was launched on 10 May 2025 (after Fajr prayer)

        Seven sites in India were targeted, including the key Pathankot air base, Udhampur air base, and Gujarat air base. Rajasthan air base and Brahmos storage site.

        The operation was launched in retaliation for Indian missile strikes that hit several locations inside Pakistan.

         ****

        132

        Kulbushan Jadev, an Indian spy, was arrested from ______?

        Correct Answer: Balochistan
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Kulbhushan Sudhir Jadhav is an Indian national.

        It is alleged by the Pakistani government that he was arrested in the Pakistani province of Balochistan 

        was arrested on charges of terrorism and spying for India's intelligence agency, the Research and Analysis Wing.

        133

        The Shortest-Serving Governor in Sindh’s History is?

        Correct Answer: Justice(R) Saeed U zaman Saddiqi
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Saeed Uz Zaman Siddiqui 31st Governor of Sindh from 11 November 2016 till 11 January 2017.

        Saeed Uz Zaman was the 15th Chief Justice of Pakistan.
        134

        Pakistan's Gawadar part is being operated by a state-run Chinese company for a period of _____ years.

        Correct Answer: 40
        Show explanation
        Explanation

        Pakistan's Gwadar Port is operated by China Overseas Port Holding Company.

        The port operations were handed over to the Chinese firm for 40 years under a long-term lease agreement.

        135

        Karakorum Range stretches from west to east in Kashmir and Gilgit with the borders of _____?

        Correct Answer: China
        Show explanation
        Explanation
        • The Karakoram Highway (KKH) is also known as the Friendship Highway in China and N-35 in Pakistan.
          • It was built by the governments of Pakistan and China and completed in 1979.
          • Starting from Hassan Abdal, it spans 1,300 (810 miles) km in Pakistan.
          • The Silk Route is also referred to as the Karakoram Highway.
          • The Karakoram Range, located between China and Pakistan.
          • It is traversed by the Karakoram Highway.
          • It connects the two countries via the Khunjerab Pass.
          136

          16th August, 1946 was declared as direct action day by?

          Correct Answer: Muslim League
          Show explanation
          Explanation

          Direct Action Day, also known as the 1946 Calcutta Killings, was a day of nationwide communal riots by the Indian Muslim community announced by Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

          It led to large-scale violence between Muslims and Hindus in the city of Calcutta (now known as Kolkata) in the Bengal province of British.

          137

          Who is the real founder of Muslim rule in northern India?

          Correct Answer: Shahab-ud-Din Muhammad Ghori
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • Shahabuddin Ghori is the founder of the Islamic State in India.
          • ***
          • Sultan Muhammad Ghori was born in 1162.
          • Shahabuddin Ghori was a warrior of Turkish origin who traveled from Ghor, Afghanistan, and conquered Delhi in 1192. 
          •  He was the true founder of Muslim rule in India.
          • ***
          • Muhammad Ghori was the ruler of the Ghori dynasty, invaded India in the year 1206 C.E and their rule extended until 1526 C.E
          138

          The CPEC project was officially launched in which year?

          Correct Answer: April 2015
          Show explanation
          Explanation
          • The China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) was officially launched in April 2015 with an initial value of $46 billion.
            • It aims to enhance regional connectivity through investments in transportation, energy, and industrial development.
            • It contributing to economic rationalization in a globalized world.
            139

            Who were the trusted commanders of Muhammad bin Qasim?

            Correct Answer: Hakim Shaibani
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Hakim Shaibani was a key lieutenant under Muhammad bin Qasim during the conquest of Sindh in the early 8th century.

             Muhammad bin Qasim sent his trusted commander Abu Hakim Shaibani toward Kanauj 

            Additional information 

            Muhammad ibn Qasim led the Muslim conquest of Sindh

            From Raja Dahir in the battle of Aror.

            He was the first Muslim to have successfully captured Hindu territories

            And initiated the early Islamic India in 712 AD.

            140

            Thal Desert is situated between ____?

            Correct Answer: Jhelum and Indus
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Thal Desert is situated in the Bhakkar District of Punjab.

            It is located between the Jhelum and Sindh rivers near the Pothohar PlateauIts.

            The Length of the Thal desert is 190 miles.

            It is called the Friendly Desert and Great Indian Desert.

            It is the largest and the National Desert of Pakistan.

            It is the 16th largest desert on the planet

            15% located in Pakistan.

            85% located in India

            Famous for: Sand and Coal 


            Additional information:

            Pakistan hosts five major deserts which are historic forests.

            1.       Cholistan (Bahawalpur, Punjab)

            2.       Thal (Bkhar district of Punjab)

            3.       Katpana desert (Sakardu)

            4.       Kharan desert (Balochistan)

            5.       Thar desert (Sindh)

            In KPK there is no desert

            The northern area of Pakistan is known as the “Deserts Free Area”.

            141

            Most of the plain areas of Balochistan are irrigated by two canals dug out from _____?

            Correct Answer: Guddu barrage
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Patfeeder Canal and Kirthar canal dug out from Guddu Barrage.

            Guddu is a barrage on the Indus River near Kashmore.

            Iskander Mirza laid the foundation-stone of Guddu Barrage in 1957, inaugurated by Ayub Khan in 1962.

            142

            President General Ayub Khan ruled for almost _____?

            Correct Answer: 10 years
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            General Ayub Khan ruled Pakistan for 10 years and 224 days (Oct 27, 1958 – Mar 25, 1969).

            He was the second President of Pakistan.

            Came to power through a military coup against President Iskandar Ali Mirza.

            Ruled as a military dictator during his tenure.

            Resigned in 1969 due to widespread protests across the country.

            143

            The biggest copper deposits were discovered in _____?

            Correct Answer: Saindak
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The discovery of gold, copper, and silver deposits at Saindak was made in the 1970s.

            Trial production started in 1995.

            The Saindak Copper-Gold project, located in Chaghi, Balochistan, operates in collaboration with China.

            144

            Which of the following receives rains more than all the other valleys?

            Correct Answer: Kashmir valley
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Kashmir Valley receives the highest rainfall due to its location in the western Himalayas.

            It experiences heavy precipitation from monsoon and western disturbances.

            145

            Harappa ruins are in which province of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Punjab
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Harappa, village in eastern Punjab province, eastern Pakistan

            The ruins of Harappa are situated in   Sahiwal District, Punjab, Pakistan

            The Bronze Age Harappan civilization, now more often called the Indus Valley Civilisation

            Harappa is small crossroads town of 15,000 people today.

            NA23-7-2023


            146

            In plateaus, irrigation is managed by _____?

            Correct Answer: Karez
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            Karez is a traditional underground irrigation system used in dry, plateau regions.
            It channels water from foothills through underground tunnels to farmlands, especially in Balochistan.
            147

            After independence the Father of the Nation, Quaid e Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah, served for ______ and died on 11th September 1948.

            Correct Answer: One year and 25 days
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was sworn in as the first Governor-General of Pakistan on 14th August 1947.

            He was served until his death on 11th September 1948—a period of 1 year and 25 days.

            He was born on 25th December 1876 in Karachi.

            148

            Who sold Kashmir to India?

            Correct Answer: Maharaja Hari Singh
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Maharaja Hari Singh, the Hindu ruler of Jammu and Kashmir, signed the Instrument of Accession with India on October 26, 1947, following tribal invasions supported by Pakistan.

            This treaty granted India control over Kashmir’s defense, communications, and foreign affairs.

            149

            Who under the treaty of Amritsar in March 1846 Purchased Kashmir?

            Correct Answer: Gulab Singh
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Treaty of Amritsar, executed by the British East India Company and Raja Gulab Singh of Jammu.

            First Anglo-Sikh War, established the princely state of Jammu and Kashmir under the suzerainty of the British Indian Empire.

            150

            Which were the main parties in the elections of 1970?

            Correct Answer: Both A and B
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The first-ever general elections were held on 7th December 1970.

            Yahya Khan issued a Legal Framework Order for the first general elections.


            300 of the 313 seats in the National Assembly

            of which 162 were in East Pakistan and 138 in West Pakistan.

            A further thirteen seats were reserved for women (seven of which were in East Pakistan and six of which were in West Pakistan)

            LeaderSheikh Mujibur RehmanZulfikar Ali Bhutto
            PartyALPPP
            Seats won167 86
            Popular vote12, 937, 1626, 148, 923
            Percentage39.20%18.63%


            151

            How many people died in the 2005 earthquake in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: More than 80000
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The earthquake of October 2005 was result of Tectonic dislocation.

            The 2005 Kashmir earthquake occurred at 08:50:39 Pakistan Standard Time on 8 October in Pakistani-administered Azad Kashmir.

            Magnitude: 7.6 Mw

            More than 80,000 people perished as a result of the quake.

            While an estimated 4 million others were left homeless.

            152

            Who has become the youngest assistant commissioner of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Rao Talha Javed
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Rao Talha Javed has set a new record by becoming the youngest assistant commissioner of Pakistan.

            He belongs to Lodhran.

            He passed his matriculation exam from Duniapur Public School with distinction in 2015 and then he did his FSc in 2017.

            He did BS Civil Engineering in 2021 and passed his CSS exam in his first attempt in 2022.

            He completed his training in the 51st Common Programme and formally joined his official duty in 2025.

            *******

            153

            Which personality was impressed by Khushal Khattak?

            Correct Answer: Allama Iqbal
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Allama Iqbal, the renowned philosopher-poet of South Asia, was deeply inspired by Khushal Khan Khattak (the 17th-century Pashtun warrior-poet).

            Iqbal praised Khattak’s poetry and leadership in his works, notably calling him the "Poet of the Afghan Nation" and referencing his resistance against Mughal oppression.
            154

            The event that led to separation of East Pakistan in 1971 was ______?

            Correct Answer: Civil war and military conflict
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The separation of East Pakistan in 1971 was triggered by political and military conflict between East and West Pakistan.

            It culminated in the Bangladesh Liberation War, leading to the creation of Bangladesh.

            155

            Pakistan experiences frequent earthquakes _______?

            Correct Answer: Due to its location on tectonic plate boundaries
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan lies near the boundary of the Indian and Eurasian tectonic plates, making it seismically active.

            The collision of these plates causes frequent earthquakes and geological activity.

            156

            The primary reason for the creation of Pakistan in 1947 was _____?

            Correct Answer: Religious freedom for Muslims
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan was created to provide a separate homeland for Muslims of the Indian subcontinent.

            The aim was to protect their religious, cultural, and political rights in a Hindu-majority India.

            157

            In 2020, Pakistan launched its first-ever metro train service in the city of _____?

            Correct Answer: Lahore
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan's first metro train, Orange Line, was launched in Lahore in 2020.

            It is a 27 km (17-mile) rapid transit system.

            Inaugurated on October 25, 2020.

            Built with Chinese collaboration.

            Inaugurated by Punjab CM Sardar Usman Buzdar.

            158

            How many river systems are there in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Five
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan has five major river systems: Indus, Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, and Sutlej.

            These rivers form the backbone of Pakistan’s irrigation and water resources.


            Rivers of Pakistan with length and province

            Sr#River NameProvinceLength Final
            1Sutlej RiverPunjab1450 km
            2Chenab RiverPunjab960 km
            3Jhelum RiverPunjab725 km
            4Ravi RiverPunjab720 km
            5Beas RiverPunjab250 km
            6Indus RiverSindh2900 km
            7Lyari RiverSindh50 km
            8Malir RiverSindh20 km
            9Gujjhar RiverSindh7 km
            10Hingol RiverBalochistan560 km
            11Dasht RiverBalochistan430 km
            12Zhob RiverBalochistan410 km
            13Dori RiverBalochistan320 km
            14Kundar RiverBalochistan166 km
            15Hub RiverBalochistan134 km
            16Mula RiverBalochistan22 km

             Bolan RiverBalochistan 
             Porali RiverBalochistan 
             Basol RiverBalochistan 
             Kech RiverBalochistan 
             Nall RiverBalochistan 
             Sukleji RiverBalochistan 
             Gilgit RiverGB KPK240 km
             Kunhar RiverGB KPK166 km
             Shigar RiverGB KPK62 km
             Astor RiverGB KPK 
            17Kabul RiverKPK700 km
            18Gomal RiverKPK400 km
            19Gambila River/Tochi RiverKPK322 km
            20Kurram RiverKPK320 km
            21Swat RiverKPK240 km
            22PanjkoraKPK220 km
            23Hunza RiverKPK190 km
            24Haro RiverKPK120 km
             Panjnad River merger of the five rivers of the Punjab, namely Jhelum, Chenab, Ravi, Beas and Sutlej71 km
            159

            How many kinds of birds are found in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: 660
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan is home to about 660 species of birds, showing rich avian diversity.

            These include resident, migratory, and endangered bird species across various habitats.

            160

            The population of Jammu & Kashmir according to 1941 census was _____?

            Correct Answer: 3 million
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In the last census of British India in 1941, the total population of Kashmir and Jammu was 3,945,000.

            1.          The total Muslim population was 2,997,000 (75.97%),

            2.          The Hindu population was 808,000 (20.48%),

            3.          The Sikh 55,000 (1.39%)

            161

            According to the first census of 1951, what was the approximate population of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: 35 million
            Show explanation
            Explanation


            162

            Which of the following is NOT a book written by Khushal Khan Khattak?

            Correct Answer: Shahnama
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Shahnama was written by Ferdowsi, not Khushal Khan Khattak.

            Dastarnama, Baznama, and Tibb Nama — are attributed to Khushal Khan Khattak.

            163

            Ruth Pfau is known for her contributions in which field in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Health
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Dr. Ruth Pfau

            • Date of Birth: 9 September 1929
            • Date of Death: 10 August 2017
            • Other Name: Mother Teresa of Pakistan (due to her work for leprosy patients)

            Major Awards:

            • Hilal-e-Imtiaz (Pakistan)
            • Hilal-e-Pakistan
            • Nishan-e-Quaid-i-Azam
            • Staufer Medal (Germany)
            • Dame of the Order of St. Gregory the Great (by Pope)
            164

            Which leader played an important role in convincing the people of Baluchistan to join the Muslim League?

            Correct Answer: Mir Jafar Khan Jamali
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Mir Jafar Khan Jamali, a prominent Baloch leader, played a pivotal role in mobilizing support for the Muslim League in Balochistan during the Pakistan Movement.

            He worked closely with Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah and helped convince the people of Balochistan to join Pakistan.

            165

            When did Fatima Jinnah become the head of the women’s wing of the All India Muslim League?

            Correct Answer: 1938
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Fatima Jinnah became the head of the women's wing of the All India Muslim League in 1938.

            She played a significant role in mobilizing women for the cause of Pakistan and the political activities of the Muslim League.

            166

            Who among the following served as an Air Commodore in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Cecil Chaudhry
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Cecil Chaudhry was a distinguished Pakistani fighter pilot.

            He served as an Air Commodore in the Pakistan Air Force.

            He was also a war hero in the 1965 and 1971 wars.

            He was also later became an educationist and human rights activist.

            167

            When did the Shahi Jirga of Baluchistan decide to join Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: 29 June 1947
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The province's shahi jirga (royal council) decided to join Pakistan on 29 June 1947.

            The princely states except Kalat acceded to Pakistan.

            168

            What are the boundaries of a map called?

            Correct Answer: Margin
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The margin refers to the boundary or edge of a map, framing the content within.

            It usually contains important map information like title, scale, and legend placement.

            169

            Begum Jahan Ara Shah Nawaz wrote several pamphlets on which of the following topics?

            Correct Answer: Educational and social issues
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Begum Jahan Ara Shah Nawaz focused on the rights and upliftment of women in her writings.

            She addressed educational and social reforms to promote gender equality and empowerment.

            Born: 1896 | Died: 1979

            170

            When did Fatima Jinnah joined All India Muslim league?

            Correct Answer: 1939
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Fatima Jinnah: younger sister of Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah

            Fatima Jinnah born: 30th July 1893

            Fatima Jinnah is referred as: Madr-e-Millat (Mother of Nation)

            Fatima Jinnah died: 9 July 1967

            Fatima Jinnah died at age: 71

            Fatima Jinnah died: Karachi

            Fatima Jinnah profession: Dental Surgeon

            171

            Who established the Muslim League in Balochistan on the instructions of Quaid-e-Azam in 1939?

            Correct Answer: Qazi Muhammad Isa
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Qazi Muhammad Isa established the Muslim League in Balochistan in 1939 on the instructions of Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah.

            He served as the President of All-India Muslim League's Balochistan branch.

            172

            According to 1941 Census ______ of the population of Kashmir state was Muslim.

            Correct Answer: 77%
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In the last census of British India in 1941, the total population of Kashmir and Jammu was 3,945,000.

            1.          The total Muslim population was 2,997,000 (75.97%),

            2.          The Hindu population was 808,000 (20.48%),

            3.          The Sikh 55,000 (1.39%)

            173

            According to UNO report Pakistan is at _____ position in the list of countries facing water crises.

            Correct Answer: 7th
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            According to some sources, Pakistan is in the top 10 countries facing water scarcity, and specifically, the 7th position according to the UNO report.

            Other reports place Pakistan at 3rd or 14th among countries with high water risk. 

            174

            After the reading lesson women's role in the Pakistan movement. It is inferred that the role of women expended because of _____?

            Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam who stressed the importance of women as equal partner of men in the struggle for a separate homeland
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Quaid-e-Azam emphasized that women were equal partners in the movement for Pakistan.

            His encouragement led to a broader and more active role of women in the independence struggle.

            175

            Which of the following opposed the Pakistan membership in United Nation?

            Correct Answer: Afghanistan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Afghanistan opposed Pakistan’s membership in the United Nations at the time of Independence:

            ****


            Afghanistan was the only country to vote against Pakistan's accession to the United Nations after its independence


            Pakistan officially joined the United Nations (UN) on 30 September 1947

            Pakistan has been elected seven times (tied with Colombia and India) into the UN Security Council, with the most recent term in 2013.

            176

            Bicameral Legislature system was introduced for the first time in Pakistan in ___?

            Correct Answer: Constitution of 1973
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            From 1947 to 1973, Pakistan had a unicameral legislature with only the National Assembly.

            The 1973 Constitution introduced a bicameral system, adding the Senate alongside the National Assembly.

            177

            Which is Pakistan 2nd largest foreign exchange earner crop?

            Correct Answer: Rice
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Rice is Pakistan's second-largest foreign exchange earner crop after cotton.
          • Pakistan ranks as the 11th largest rice producer globally.
          • It is the 5th largest rice exporter in the world.
            • 178

            Artificial Forests being maintained in ______?

            Correct Answer: Changa Manga
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • Changa Manga is one of the largest man-made forests in Pakistan, located in Punjab.
          • It was originally planted during the British colonial era to provide timber for railway networks.
            • 179

            Chandka Medical college is located at ______?

            Correct Answer: Larkana
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Chandka is the old name of Larkana.

            Larakana is also called city of Guavas.

            Larkana is a city located in Sindh.

            180

            Bala Hisar fort built by _________?

            Correct Answer: Babur
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Babur first built a fort here in 1526 after capturing Peshawar.

            First mentioned by 7th-century explorer Xuanzang, the fort was used as a royal residence for the Durrani Empire since December 1747.

            The Maratha Empire captured it after the Battle of Peshawar in 1758.

            The Sikhs reconstructed the fort after capturing Peshawar in March 1823.

            181

            The biggest market of Pakistan export and import is ______?

            Correct Answer: USA
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The biggest market for Pakistan's exports is the United States, 

            Top Export Items: Textiles (house linens, knitwear), rice, and leather products.


            Additional information

            Imports: China (largest)

            Import Items: Petroleum products (gas, crude, refined), palm oil, and machinery. 

            182

            Which division "Swat" District belongs to?

            Correct Answer: Malakand
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Swat District also known as the Swat Valley, is a district in the Malakand Division of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan

            Swat is located in the Hindu Kush mountain range in KPK  

            Swat is the 15th-largest district of Khyber Pakhtunkhwa

            KNOWN ABOUT SWAT

            Swat was merged with Pakistan in 1849

            On 28 July 1969, General Yahya Khan announced the full integration states of Chitral, Dir, and Swat.

            Capital: Saidu Sharif

            Swat is called the Switzerland of Pakistan because of its snow-capped mountains and beautiful scenery.

            Mingora is the largest city of Swat Valley

            Gwalerai is a famous city for apples in Swat

            183

            Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk prepared ______?

            Correct Answer: The points of Simla Deputation
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Nawab Mohsin-ul-Mulk played a key role in drafting the demands of the Simla Deputation (1906).

            He was a pivotal moment in the political awakening of Muslims in British India.

            The delegation, led by Aga Khan III, met Lord Minto (the Viceroy) and presented demands for separate electorates and safeguards for Muslim political rights.

            184

            The most important and famous Urdu newspaper published from Lahore by Maulana Zafar Ali khan?

            Correct Answer: Zamindar
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            The founder of ‘Zamindar’ (newspaper) was Maulana Zafar Ali Khan.
            He is generally considered to be "the father of Urdu journalism."
            185

            The Silver Fibre of Pakistan is ________?

            Correct Answer: Cotton
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Cotton is known as the "Silver Fibre" of Pakistan due to its economic importance and export value.

            It plays a key role in Pakistan’s textile industry, the backbone of the country's economy.

            186

            Who is known as the King of Qawwali?

            Correct Answer: Ustad Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan born October 1948.

            King of Qawwali, Nusrat Fateh Ali Khan died on August 16, 1997 of a sudden cardiac arrest in London at the age of 48.

            187

            Who was the first Mughal Emperor to allow Britishers to establish trade links with India?

            Correct Answer: Jahangir
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Jahangir was the first Mughal Emperor to allow Britishers to establish trade links with India.
            • He gave permission for trade to the British East India Company, allowing them to establish a factory at Surat in 1613.
            • This marked the beginning of British trade in India during the Mughal era.
            188

            According to Iqbal's philosophy it is necessary for a student ______?

            Correct Answer: Not look upon facts from others points of view
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            According to Iqbal’s philosophy, a student should think independently and not just accept others’ viewpoints blindly.

            This emphasizes self-thinking and personal understanding rather than following others.

            189

            Sir Syed Ahmad Khan retired from the British Service as ________?

            Correct Answer: Judge
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In 1838, Syed Ahmad entered the service of East India Company and went on to become a judge at a Small Causes Court in 1867, retiring from 1876. 


            *******

            • Got Knowledge From Farid Ud Din (Maternal-grandfather – Ex Mughal Pm)
            • Joined Gov’t In 1839  => Clerical Job naib munshi (administrative assistant)
            • Promoted As Sub-judge 1841
            • Transferred To Delhi  Chief Judge In 1846 
            • Member Of Imperial Council 1886



            • Aligarh Movement Objective:
            • 1. Modern Education For Muslims To Compete Hindus 
            • 2. Cooperation With The British Government


            • Established:

            • 1. Schools   • Muradabad (1859)  • Ghazipur (1863)
            • 2. Scientific Society At Gahazipur (1864)
            • 3. Muhammadan Educational Conference 1866
            • 4. Muhammadan Anglo-oriental College =>
            • Mao High School was Established in 1875
            • Got Status Of College 1877  
            • Status Of University 1920
            190

            In 1859 Sir Syed Ahmed Khan established/set up a first school where Persian and English were taught?

            Correct Answer: Muradabad
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • In 1859, Sir Syed Ahmed Khan established a school in Muradabad, where Persian and English were taught.
          • Later, he founded the Mohammedan Anglo-Oriental College in Aligarh in 1875, which became Aligarh Muslim University.
            • 191

            During Alla-ud-Din period Mongols invaded Delhi at least ____ times between 1296 to 1306 AD. But each time they were repulsed.

            Correct Answer: Six
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            During Alla-ud-Din Khilji's reign (1296-1306 AD), the Mongols invaded Delhi at least six times.
