            Despite their repeated invasions, Alla-ud-Din successfully repulsed them each time, demonstrating his military strength.

            192

            Which was a time, when man began making tools and weapons from metals. This period was 2500 BC to 1500 BC?

            Correct Answer: Bronze age
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Bronze Age (2500 BC to 1500 BC) is when humans started making tools and weapons using bronze, an alloy of copper and tin.

            It marked significant advancements in metallurgy and technology.

            It leading to improvements in agriculture, warfare, and trade.

            193

            The British established an organization "The East India Company" for trade purposes and work about how many years for the settlement?

            Correct Answer: 250
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The East India Company (EIC) was established in 1600 and effectively dissolved in 1858 after the Indian Rebellion of 1857.

            Duration: ~258 years

            Key Phases:

            1600–1757: Trade monopoly (spices, textiles).

            1757–1858: Political control (Battle of Plassey to Crown rule).

            194

            What was the approximate population of Pakistan in the year 2010?

            Correct Answer: 173.51 million
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             In 2010, the estimated population of Pakistan was approximately 173.51 

            195

            The Faisal Mosque and International Islamic University were built with the financial support of ______?

            Correct Answer: Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The mosque was built by a Saudi King named King Faisal who gifted it in recognition of friendship between Pakistan and Saudi Arabia.

            Faisal Mosque of Pakistan was opened to the public in 1986 OR 1987

            **

            On 18th June 1988 the first prayer  was held in the Faisal mosque

            **

            The Faisal Mosque is the largest mosque in Pakistan,

            located in the national capital city of Islamabad.

            Completed in 1986,

            it was designed by Turkish architect Vedat Dalokay


            196

            The upper Indus plain starts from _____?

            Correct Answer: Kalabagh
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Indus River starts in Tibet

            The river Indus originates from the Mansarovar lake

            The Indus River flows through India, Pakistan,

            The Indus River finally falls into the Arabian Sea near Karachi.

            River Indus starts making a delta near the city of Thatta

            The old name of the Indus river was: Sindhu

            National River of Pakistan: Indus River

            6 Barrages constructed in Indus River

            Indus River Length: 3,180 km

            Indus River 2nd source: Gar Tsangpo

            The upper Indus plain ends at Mithankot 

            The upper Indus plain starts from Kalabagh

            ND11-2-2023


            197

            The _____ were/was the founder of Vedic Culture?

            Correct Answer: Aryan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Aryans are considered the founders of Vedic culture, which emerged around 1500 BCE in India.

            They brought the Vedic texts and formed the foundation of Hinduism and early Indian civilization.

            198

            In the First Round Table, all the parties were represented except the _____?

            Correct Answer: Congress
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            The first and third Round Table conferences did not take part in the Indian National Congress.

            *First round table conference: 1930 and January 1931 in London
            The Indian National Congress decided not to participate
            As Many of the INC leaders were imprisoned due to their involvement in the civil disobedience movement.

            *3rd round table conference: 17 Nov 1932 and 24 Dec 1932
            The Indian National Congress was not invited to this table conference 
            Because the recommendations of this conference were published in a White Paper in 1933.

            Additional informational 
            The Round Table Conference was a series of Three Conference 
            Conducted by the Labour Party-led British government 
            To discuss constitutional reforms in British India during 1930-32
            The second Round Table conference was held from 7 Sep 1931 to 1 Dec 1931
            199

            In which movement the congress leader Mahatma Gandhi announced to support the Muslims completely?

            Correct Answer: Khilafat Movement
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Mahatma Gandhi supported the Khilafat Movement (1919–1924) to gain Muslim support against British rule.

            It was a joint Hindu-Muslim effort to protest the dismemberment of the Ottoman Caliphate after World War I.

            200

            When the recommendations of the Simon Commission were rejected by the Indians Public, a British Minister _____ passed insulting remarks that the Indian political parties were not able to present any proposals.

            Correct Answer: Lord Birkenhead
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Lord Birkenhead, the British Secretary of State for India, insulted Indian leaders, claiming they couldn't agree on a constitution.

            His remarks prompted Indian leaders to form the Nehru Report (1928) as a counter-proposal.

            201

            Who is called the prisoner of Malta?

            Correct Answer: Maulana Muhammad-ul-Hassan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Maulana Muhammad-ul-Hassan (also known as Hasrat Mohani) was famously called the Prisoner of Malta due to his imprisonment by the British.

            He was jailed for his anti-British activities and later exiled to Malta, hence the title.

            202

            On which date Pakistan celebrated Youm-e-Tashakkur after giving India befitting response?

            Correct Answer: 11 May 2025
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            PM Shahbaz Sharif announced Youm-e-Tashakkur to celebrate success of 'Operation Bunyan-um-Marsoos’ on 11th May 2025 wherein Pakistan gave befitting response to Indian aggression.

            Youm-e-Tashakkur is called thanks giving day.


             Additional Information: 

            This operation was launched on 10 May 2025 (after Fajr prayer)

            ***
            203

            General Elections for ______ legislature were held in 1945.

            Correct Answer: Central
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The 1945 general elections in British India were held for the Central Legislature (Central Legislative Assembly).

            The General Election of 1945-46 was held in 11 provinces of British India.

            It included the provinces under the British rule, excluding princely states.

            Lord Wavell was the Viceroy of India at the time.

            204

            The Cripps Mission proposed ______ for India.

            Correct Answer: Independence at the end of WW-II
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Cripps Mission (1942) proposed that India would be granted full independence after the Second World War.

            It also offered the right to provinces to opt out, which was rejected by major Indian political parties.

            205

            Sir Syed Ahmad Khan, in the early phase of his politics, was a strong supporter of _______?

            Correct Answer: Hindu-Muslim Unity
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In the early phase of his political career, Sir Syed Ahmad Khan strongly advocated for Hindu-Muslim unity to ensure cooperation and peace under British rule.

            He believed both communities could work together for the progress of India before later emphasizing Muslim identity separately.

            206

            Pakistan and Iran were members of ______?

            Correct Answer: ECO, RCD, and D-8
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan and Iran were members of RCD (Regional Cooperation for Development) formed in 1964, and later ECO (Economic Cooperation Organization), which succeeded RCD in 1985.

            Both are also members of D-8 (Developing-8), an organization for development cooperation among 8 Muslim-majority countries.

            207

            The oldest known book of the Pashto language is considered to be ______?

            Correct Answer: Pata Khazana
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pata Khazana is considered the oldest known book in the Pashto language, claimed to date back to the 18th century.

            It is a manuscript compiling biographies and poetry of earlier Pashto poets.

            208

            Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah was appointed as First Governor General of Pakistan on ______ August 1947.

            Correct Answer: 14th
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Quaid e Azam worked as governor-general of Pakistan for 13 months

            from 14 August 1947 – 11 September 1948

            He was the 1st Governor-General of Pakistan.

            Succeeded by: Khawaja Nazimuddin


            Additional information:

            Muhammad Ali Jinnah joined the league in 1913.

            He was 37 years old when he joined Muslim League.

            He joined the Indian National Congress in 1906 and left in 1920

            He was Born on December 25, 1876, in Karachi

            He died on September 11, 1948, in Karachi

            209

            Who wrote Kitab Tarikh-i-Awila in the 13th century?

            Correct Answer: Bayazid Ansari
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Bayazid Ansari, a 13th-century scholar, wrote Kitab Tarikh-i-Awila.

            It is a historical text focusing on the early history of the region.

            210

            Who was the President of Pakistan, removed by General Pervez Musharraf in June 2001?

            Correct Answer: Rafiq Tarar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Rafique Tarar, the former President of Pakistan, resigned during Pervez Musharraf's presidency.


            History

            Muhammad Rafiq Tarar served as the ninth president of Pakistan 

            The presidential term of Muhammad Rafiq Tarar: January 1, 1998 – June 20, 2001

            He was the Member of the Senate of Pakistan from (1997–1998)

            He served as a senior justice of the Supreme Court of Pakistan from 1991 to 1994

            He was the 28th Chief Justice of Lahore High Court from 1989 to 1991

            He  was born on November 1929 – died on 7 March 2022

            211

            How many letters were exchanged during the Jinnah-Gandhi talks?

            Correct Answer: 24
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Jinnah-Gandhi talks occurred from September 9 to September 27, 1944.

            During this period, 24 letters were exchanged between Muhammad Ali Jinnah and Mahatma Gandhi.

            212

            Maulvi Fazlul Haq was from the province of ______?

            Correct Answer: Bengal
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Maulvi Fazlul Haq was a prominent leader from Bengal.

            He played a significant role in the political landscape of British India.

            He is often referred to as the "Sher-e-Bengal" (Tiger of Bengal).

            213

            What event in 1867 changed Sir Syed's viewpoint on Indian nationhood?

            Correct Answer: Hindi-Urdu controversy
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Hindi-Urdu controversy of 1867 significantly changed Sir Syed Ahmed Khan's viewpoint on Indian nationhood.

            This controversy highlighted the growing divide between Hindus and Muslims.

            It influencing Sir Syed to focus on promoting education and social reforms among Muslims, leading to his support for a separate identity for Muslims in India.

            214

            What significant change did the British make to Mughal coins under Akbar II?

            Correct Answer: Removed Persian text
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Under Akbar II, the British removed Persian inscriptions from the Mughal coins.

            This was part of their effort to reduce Mughal authority and establish British supremacy in India.

            215

            Which empire had control over Gandhara from 486 BCE to 522 BCE?

            Correct Answer: Persian Empire
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Achaemenid Persian Empire ruled over Gandhara from around 522 BCE to 486 BCE, during the reign of Darius I.

            Gandhara became a satrapy (province) of the Persian Empire after being annexed under Darius’s expansion into the Indian subcontinent.

            216

            Which government is responsible for removing encroachments from public roads and organizing cattle markets and local fairs?

            Correct Answer: Local Government
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Local Government is responsible for managing municipal affairs, including removing encroachments from public roads and organizing local events such as cattle markets and fairs.

            They handle administrative duties related to local development and public services within a specific region or locality.

            217

            Which ministry was Sardar Nishtar appointed to in 1946?

            Correct Answer: Communications
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Muslim League Ministers in the Interim Government of 1946:

            • Liaquat Ali Khan – Finance (Introduced the "Poor Man's Budget")
            • I. I. Chundrigar – Commerce
            • Abdur Rab Nishtar – Communications
            • Ghazanfar Ali Khan – Health
            • Jogendra Nath Mandal – Law (Legislature)

            Liaquat Ali Khan's "Poor Man's Budget" focused on redistributing wealth and addressing the economic disparity between the rich and the poor.

            218

            Pakistan has been elected as a non-permanent member of which UN body?

            Correct Answer: Security Council
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan has been elected as a non-permanent member of the United Nations Security Council (UNSC) for the 2025–2026 term.

            This marks the eighth time Pakistan has secured a seat on the 15-member council.

            It is responsible for maintaining international peace and security.

            219

            Who currently runs most of the courier services in different cities of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Private companies
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Most courier services in Pakistan, such as TCS, Leopard, and M&P, are operated by private companies.

            They offer faster and more flexible delivery services compared to government-run options.

            220

            Which leader supported the Lahore Resolution (1940) from U.P?

            Correct Answer: Chaudhary Khaliquzzaman
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Lahore Resolution (1940), which laid the foundation for the creation of Pakistan, was supported by Chaudhary Khaliquzzaman, a prominent Muslim leader from United Provinces (U.P.) (now Uttar Pradesh).

            His advocacy made him a key figure in the 1940s Pakistan Movement

            More Details

            Other leaders listed (Maulana Abdul Hamid, Aurangzeb Khan, I.I. Chundreegar) were not directly associated with the Lahore Resolution from U.P.

            221

            What decision did Maharaja Hari Singh make that ignored the will of the people?

            Correct Answer: He decided to accede to India
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Maharaja Hari Singh of Jammu and Kashmir decided to accede to India in 1947, ignoring the will of the people, many of whom favored joining Pakistan.

            This decision led to significant political and military conflict between India and Pakistan over the Kashmir region.

            222

            Which ancient civilization is associated with Tunic and Toga?

            Correct Answer: Ancient Rome
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The tunic and toga were both distinctive garments worn in Ancient Rome.

            The tunic was a basic garment, and the toga was a formal attire worn by Roman citizens, especially for public events.

            223

            Which national day in Pakistan was initiated by Qazi Hussain Ahmad?

            Correct Answer: Kashmir Solidarity Day
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Kashmir Solidarity Day on 5th February was initiated by Qazi Hussain Ahmad in 1990 to express support for the Kashmiri people.

            It became an official public holiday in Pakistan, observed annually to highlight the Kashmir issue.

            224

            Who presented the first resolution in favor of an independent Muslim state in the Sindh Assembly?

            Correct Answer: Sheikh Abdul Majeed Sindhi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The first resolution in favor of an independent Muslim state in the Sindh Assembly was presented by Sheikh Abdul Majeed Sindhi.

            He proposed that the All India Muslim League create a constitutional scheme for Muslims to achieve full independence.

            225

            Which of the following tributaries is not part of the Indus river system?

            Correct Answer: Porali
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Porali River is not a part of the Indus River system; it flows in Balochistan and drains into the Arabian Sea separately.

            Bolan, Chenab, and Panjkora are all tributaries of the Indus River system in Pakistan.

            226

            “Musalman's are a nation according to any definition of nation and they must have their own homeland.” These are the words of ______?

            Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Jinnah in his speech espoused the ‘two-nation theory’, saying: 

            “Mussalmans are a nation according to any definition of nation, and they must have their own homeland, their territory, and their state...

            We wish our people to develop to the fullest spiritual, cultural, economic, social and political life in a way that we think best and in consonance with our own ideals and according to the genius of our people”. 

            227

            What was one of the key reasons for the rift between Nawaz Sharif and General Musharraf before the 1999 coup?

            Correct Answer: Kargil conflict
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            One of the key reasons for the rift between Nawaz Sharif and General Pervez Musharraf before the 1999 coup was the Kargil conflict.

            Nawaz Sharif was unhappy with the way the conflict was handled, particularly the lack of proper consultation with him by the military leadership, which led to tensions.

            228

            Who initiated the Commission on National Education in Pakistan in 1959?

            Correct Answer: General Ayub Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Sharif Commission, also known as the National Commission on Education.

            It was initiated by the government of President Muhammad Ayub Khan in 1959.

            This commission was formed with the goal of reforming Pakistan's educational system to better meet the needs of the nation.

            The commission was headed by S.M. Sharif.

            229

            سید محمد راشد روضہ دھنی رحمۃ اللہ علیہ کہاں پیدا ہوئے؟

            Correct Answer: خیرپور
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            سید محمد راشد روضہ دھنی رحمۃ اللہ علیہ خیرپور میں پیدا ہوئے۔

            وہ سندھ کے شہر خیرپور میرس کے رہائشی تھے۔

            ان کی زندگی علم و عمل کا بہترین نمونہ تھی اور انہوں نے عوام کی رہنمائی کے لیے اہم کردار ادا کیا۔

            230

            What type of state was Pakistan envisioned as in the 1956 Constitution?

            Correct Answer: A welfare state
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The 1956 Constitution declared Pakistan as an Islamic republic aiming to be a welfare state.

            It emphasized social justice, equality, and the well-being of all citizens regardless of religion or background.

            231

            Barrages have helped convert which type of land into fertile and productive areas?

            Correct Answer: Barren land
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Barrages control river water and divert it for irrigation.

            This helps convert barren land into fertile and productive agricultural areas.

            232

            Which empire ruled Gandhara from 522–486 BCE?

            Correct Answer: Iranians
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Gandhara was ruled by the Achaemenid Empire (Iranians) during 522–486 BCE, under Darius I.

            They included Gandhara as a satrapy (province) in their vast empire.

            233

            When the Pakistan Movement was at its peak, who ruled over Gilgit-Baltistan?

            Correct Answer: Maharaja of Kashmir
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            At the peak of the Pakistan Movement, Gilgit-Baltistan was under the control of the Maharaja of Kashmir, as it was a part of the princely state of Jammu and Kashmir.

            In 1947, following the partition, the region was later administered by Pakistan after the Kashmir conflict.

            234

            What movement did Chaudhry Rahmat Ali start while in England?

            Correct Answer: Pakistan National Movement
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Chaudhry Rahmat Ali started the Pakistan National Movement in England in 1933.

            He is credited with coining the name "Pakistan" and advocating for a separate Muslim state.

            235

            What was the name of Qazi Hussain Ahmad’s father?

            Correct Answer: Qazi Muhammad Abdul Rab
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Born: 12 January 1938 in Nowshera, NWFP, British India.

            Died: 5 January 2013 in Islamabad, Pakistan at the age of 74.

            236

            Against whom did Syed Ahmad Shaheed lead his Jihad movement?

            Correct Answer: Sikh Ruler
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Syed Ahmad Shaheed (1786-1831) was a Muslim leader and reformer who founded the Jihad movement in India.

            He led a rebellion against the Sikh Empire in the early 19th century.

            The Jihad movement, also known as the Tariqah-i-Muhammadiyah.

            It aimed to reform Islamic practices, promote religious purification, and resist British colonial rule.

            237

            محمد بن قاسم نے سندھ میں کتنا وقت گزارا؟

            Correct Answer: 4
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            محمد بن قاسم نے 712 عیسوی میں سندھ فتح کیا اور تقریباً 4 سال وہاں حکمرانی کی۔

            اس دوران انہوں نے اسلامی نظام حکومت قائم کیا اور عدل و انصاف کا نفاذ کیا۔

            238

            Which of the following steps taken by India against Pakistan after Pahalgam attack that killed at least 26 tourists?

            Correct Answer: All of the Above
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            At least 24 people were killed in India-occupied Kashmir when gunmen opened fire on a group of tourists.

            Incident happed on April 22, 2025.

            The attack took place in Pahalgam, a popular destination in the scenic Muslim-majority territory.


            Additional Information: 

            After this attack, India suspended Indus Waters Treaty 

            Closed Check-post named Attari 

            Asked Pakistani nationals to leave India within 48 Hours 

            ****

            239

            ایگریکلچر اور فشریز کس صوبے میں سب سے زیادہ ہیں؟

            Correct Answer: سندھ
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            سندھ میں دریائے سندھ کی وجہ سے زرخیز زمین دستیاب ہے، جو ایگریکلچر کے لیے موزوں ہے۔

            سندھ کا ساحلی علاقہ ماہی گیری (فشریز) کے لیے اہم ہے، خاص طور پر کراچی اور ٹھٹھہ کے علاقے۔

            240

            Name the country that used Israeli-made Harop Drones to cause panic among Pakistani people in May 2025?

            Correct Answer: India
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            On 8 May 2025, India used Israeli-made Harop Drones to cause panic among Pakistani people. 


            Additional Information:

            India launched military attack on Pakistan on 7 May 2025

            Pakistani military brought down five Indian jets in retaliation.

            India targeted Kotli, Bahawalpur, Muridke, Bagh and Muzaffarabad.

            India termed this aggression as “Operation Sindoor”.

            As per Pakistan, 31 civilians killed and 46 injured.

            241

            Which of the following is the Oldest Language of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Sindhi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Sindh is one of the world's oldest languages, written in right-handed Perso-Arabic script

            ***

            30 languages are spoken in Pakistan

            while

            Five languages have more than 10 million speakers each – 

            Punjabi

             Pashto

            Sindhi

            Saraiki

            Urdu.

            while

            The most widely spoken language in Pakistan is Punjabi (48 % speak), followed by the Punjabi variant Siraiki (10 percent),

            2nd Sindhi 12%

            Pashto 8%

            Balochi 3%

            Hindko 2%

            ND18-2-2023

            RY5022023


            242

            Who held the Ministry of Finance in the Interim Government of 1946?

            Correct Answer: Liaquat Ali Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Muslim League Ministers in the Interim Government of 1946:

            • Liaquat Ali Khan – Finance (Introduced the "Poor Man's Budget")
            • I. I. Chundrigar – Commerce
            • Abdur Rab Nishtar – Communications
            • Ghazanfar Ali Khan – Health
            • Jogendra Nath Mandal – Law (Legislature)

            Liaquat Ali Khan's "Poor Man's Budget" focused on redistributing wealth and addressing the economic disparity between the rich and the poor.

            243

            صلاح الدین ایوبی کہاں پیدا ہوئے؟

            Correct Answer: تکریت
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            صلاح الدین ایوبی کا پیدائش 1137 میں تکریت (عراق) میں ہوئی تھی۔

            ان کا اصل نام یوسف بن ایوب تھا اور وہ مسلم تاریخ کے عظیم رہنماؤں میں شمار ہوتے ہیں، جنہوں نے صلیبی جنگوں میں اہم کردار ادا کیا۔

            244

            Who has become First Hindu Woman Assistant Commissioner in Balochistan?

            Correct Answer: Kashish Chaudhary
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Kashish Chaudhary has become the first Hindu woman in Balochistan to pass the Provincial Civil Services (PCS) examination, earning the prestigious position of Assistant Commissioner.

            She became AC at the age of 25.

            She belongs to Nushki.  

            Kashish’s determination and commitment have set a powerful example for women across the province.

            ****

            245

            کوڑا چھپائی کس کا کھیل ہے؟

            Correct Answer: لڑکیوں کا
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            کوڑا چھپائی ایک روایتی کھیل ہے جو خاص طور پر دیہاتی علاقوں میں لڑکیاں کھیلتی ہیں۔

            اس کھیل میں ایک شے کو چھپا کر باقی کھلاڑیوں سے ڈھونڈنے کو کہا جاتا ہے۔

            246

            Most of the _____ projects are completed under the China-Pakistan Economic Corridor in 2020.

            Correct Answer: Roads
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In 2020, most completed projects under CPEC were related to the road infrastructure, improving connectivity between Pakistan and China.

            Major road projects like Hazara Motorway and parts of the Western Route were key achievements in this sector.

            247

            Sulimani and Makrani are the two dialects of which language?

            Correct Answer: Balochi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Sulimani and Makrani are major dialects of the Balochi language, spoken in Balochistan and parts of Iran and Afghanistan.

            These dialects vary slightly in pronunciation and vocabulary but are mutually intelligible.

            248

            The first government of Mian Nawaz Sharif ended due to _____?

            Correct Answer: Dismissal by the President
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Mian Nawaz Sharif's first government (1990–1993) ended when President Ghulam Ishaq Khan dismissed him under Article 58(2)(b) of the Constitution.

            Although the Supreme Court reinstated Nawaz Sharif, he eventually resigned after pressure during a power struggle with the president.

            249

            Pakistan has supported the Rohingya Muslims cause against the Government of ______?

            Correct Answer: Myanmar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Rohingya Muslims are an ethnic minority persecuted by the Government of Myanmar.

            Pakistan has voiced support for their rights and condemned the violence against them on international platforms.

            250

            RCD became dissolve/dis-functional after Iranian Revolution in which year?

            Correct Answer: 1979
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            RCD was established on: 21 July, 1964

            RCD was dissolved in: 1979

            RCD stand for Regional Cooperation for Development

            RCD: Established by Iran, Pakistan and Turkey

            RCD headquarters: Iran

            251

            Interest free banking system was introduced in Pakistan by ______?

            Correct Answer: General Zia-ul-Haq
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            1st Jan 1981 interest-Free Banking was introduced in Pakistan first time.

            From July 1, 1985, all commercial banking in Pak Rupees was made interest-free. 

            In 1985 the President of Pakistan Mohammad Zia-ul-Haq introduced an interest-free banking system in Pakistan

            Meezan Bank is interest-free in Pakistan.

            252

            Judges of the High Courts are appointed by the _______ in Pakistan.

            Correct Answer: President
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Judges of High Courts in Pakistan are appointed by the President.

            In accordance with Article 175A of the Constitution.

            As outlined in Chapter 3 of the Constitution. 

            253

            Which team was the runner-up of Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2020?

            Correct Answer: Lahore Qalandars
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan Super League (PSL) 2020

            Edition: Fifth

            Dates: 20 Feb – 17 Nov 2020

            Administrator: PCB

            Format: Twenty20

            Venue: Pakistan

            Winner: Karachi Kings (1st title)

            Karachi Kings won by: 5 wickets

            Runner-up: Lahore Qalandars

            Participants: 6

            Matches:34

            Player of the series: Babar Azam (Karachi Kings)

            Babar Azam made: 473 runs

            Most runs: Babar Azam

            Most wickets: Shaheen Afridi (Lahore Qalandars)

            Shahid Afridi took: 17 wickets

             

            254

            Which Viceroy convened the ‘Simla Conference’ in 1945?

            Correct Answer: Lord Wavell
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Simla Conference of 1945 was a meeting between the Viceroy of India Lord Wavell and the major political leaders of British India at the Viceregal Lodge in Simla.

            Lord Wavell

            Field Marshal Archibald Percival Wavell, 1st Earl Wavell, GCB, GCSI, GCIE, CMG, MC, KStJ, PC was a senior officer of the British Army.


             He served in the Second Boer War, the Bazar Valley Campaign and the First World War, during which he was wounded in the Second Battle of Ypres

            Born: May 5, 1883, Colchester, United Kingdom

            Died: May 24, 1950, Westminster, London, United Kingdom

            Full name: Archibald Percival Wavell, 1st Earl Wavell

            255

            پاکستان میں کتنے بڑے ڈیم ہیں؟

            Correct Answer: 3
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            پاکستان میں تین بڑے ڈیم شمار کیے جاتے ہیں

            تربیلا ڈیم

            منگلا ڈیم

            دیامر بھاشا ڈیم

            256

            Emergency declared in hospitals of which Pakistani province on 8 May 2025 after Indian missile attacks?

            Correct Answer: Punjab
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            On 8 May 2025, The Punjab government declared emergency in all hospitals of the province, allocating 50 percent of the total beds to meet any emergency situation, given the Indian missile attacks in various parts of the province.


            Additional Information:

            India launched military attack on Pakistan on 7 May 2025

            Pakistani military brought down five Indian jets in retaliation.

            India targeted Kotli, Bahawalpur, Muridke, Bagh and Muzaffarabad.

            India termed this aggression as “Operation Sindoor”.

            As per Pakistan, 31 civilians killed and 46 injured.

            257

            اقبال نے مغربی تصور وطنیت کی _____ کی ہے۔

            Correct Answer: مخالفت
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            علامہ اقبال نے مغربی تصورِ وطنیت کی مخالفت کی ہے کیونکہ وہ اسے ملتِ اسلامیہ کے اتحاد کے خلاف سمجھتے تھے۔

            ان کے نزدیک اسلامی اخوت اور دین کی بنیاد پر اتحاد وطن سے زیادہ اہم ہے۔

            258

            سن 1996 میں جب افغانستان میں طالبان کی حکومت ہوئی تو پاکستان ______ ملک تھا جس نے طالبان کی حکومت تسلیم کی۔

            Correct Answer: پہلا
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            پاکستان پہلا ملک تھا جس نے 1996 میں طالبان کی حکومت کو باضابطہ طور پر تسلیم کیا۔

            اس کے بعد سعودی عرب اور متحدہ عرب امارات نے بھی طالبان حکومت کو تسلیم کیا تھا۔

            259

            کھیل تماشے، صوفیا سے محبت اور قصہ گوئی ____ کی ثقافت کا حصہ ہے۔

            Correct Answer: پنجاب
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            پنجاب کی ثقافت میں صوفی ازم، قصہ گوئی، میلوں ٹھیلوں اور کھیل تماشوں کو اہم مقام حاصل ہے۔

            صوفیا جیسے بابا فرید، بلھے شاہ اور وارث شاہ پنجاب کی ثقافتی شناخت کا حصہ ہیں۔

            260

            The source of economy for ancient civilization was _____?

            Correct Answer: Agriculture
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Agriculture was the primary economic foundation of ancient civilizations (e.g., Mesopotamia, Egypt, Indus Valley). It provided:

            • Food surplus, enabling population growth.
            • Raw materials (e.g., cotton, wool) for trade and crafts.
            • Stability required for societal development.
            261

            The Mesopotamia Civilization grew between the rivers _____?

            Correct Answer: Dajla and Furat
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Mesopotamian Civilization developed between the Tigris (Dajla) and Euphrates (Furat) rivers.

            This region is also known as the "Cradle of Civilization."

            262

            The word Hindu is derived from the Sanskrit word Sindhu, which means _____?

            Correct Answer: The land of seven rivers
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Sindhu refers to the Indus River and its surrounding region, traditionally associated with seven rivers.

            The word Hindu was later derived by Persians from Sindhu to describe the people living beyond the Indus.

            263

            Pakistan is located as a ______ between the Islamic countries.

            Correct Answer: Link
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan is strategically located as a link between Central Asia, the Middle East, and South Asia.

            It serves as a bridge for economic, cultural, and political connections among Islamic countries in these regions.

            264

            "Pakistan's Constitution should incorporate the essential principles of Islam, which are as good and relevant in our day, as we're 1300 years ago. But Pakistan should not a theocratic state rule by priests." This statement was given by ______?

            Correct Answer: Quaid-e-Azam
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            This statement reflects Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah’s vision of Pakistan as a modern Islamic state based on justice and equality.

            He emphasized Islamic principles but clearly rejected the idea of a theocratic state ruled by priests.

            265

            Which one of the following scientists is the Aristotle of the Muslim world?

            Correct Answer: Ibn sina
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Ibn Sina (Avicenna) is often called the "Aristotle of the Muslim world" for his vast contributions to philosophy and science.

            He blended Greek philosophy, especially Aristotle's ideas, with Islamic thought in his works.

            266

            The downfall of Muslim rule in India started with the demise of emperor _____?

            Correct Answer: Aurangzeb
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Muhi al-Din Muhammad, commonly known as Aurangzeb

            Aurangzeb was the sixth emperor of the Mughal Empire  ruling from July 1658 until his death in 1707

            Aurangzeb was Born on November 3, 1618, in Dahod, India

            Aurangzeb was Died on: March 3, 1707, Bhingar, Ahmednagar, India

            Aurangzeb, one of the highest debated Mughal emperors

            Unlike other rulers, Aurangzeb only had one wife.

            ****

            nd9-2-2023

            267

            The most important _____ area of Pakistan in the Indus valley.

            Correct Answer: Agriculture
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Indus Valley is the most important agriculture area of Pakistan.

            It has fertile soil and access to river water, making it ideal for farming.

            268

            Most of the water in the Northern part of subcontinent comes from ______?

            Correct Answer: Rivers
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Most water in the northern part of the subcontinent comes from Himalayan rivers like the Indus, Ganges, and Brahmaputra.

            These rivers are fed by glaciers and snowmelt, ensuring a continuous water supply.

            269

            Life in villages is called _____?

            Correct Answer: Rural life
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            "Rural life" refers to life in the countryside or villages, away from towns and cities.

            It typically includes farming, less population density, and natural surroundings.

            270

            Objective Resolution defines the role of _____ in the constitution of Pakistan.

            Correct Answer: People
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The Objective Resolution defines the role of the sovereignty of Allah/Islamic principles
              • Specifically, that sovereignty belongs to Allah, and authority is delegated to the people
              • The foundational principles of democracy, freedom, equality, and social justice 
            •  Passed on March 12, 1949, by the first Constituent Assembly.
            •  Now incorporated directly into the 1973 Constitution as Article 2A

            Last updated question NS on 09 MAY 2026

            271

            Who played an important role in Lucknow Pact?

            Correct Answer: Jinnah
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Muhammad Ali Jinnah played a key role in formulating the Lucknow Pact in 1916 between the Indian National Congress and the All-India Muslim League.

            He was instrumental in bringing Hindu-Muslim unity at that time, earning him the title "Ambassador of Hindu-Muslim Unity."

            272

            Who presided the Khilafat Conference on 23rd November 1919?

            Correct Answer: A.K Fazl-ul-Haq
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Khilafat Conference held on 23rd November 1919 in Delhi was presided over by A. K. Fazlul Huq, a prominent figure from Bengal. 

            Mahatma Gandhi was elected as President of the India Khilafat Conference (23rd November 1919).

            Khilafat Committee was formed in Bombay On 20 March 1919.

            An all-India Khilafat Conference was organized in Delhi on 23 November 1919.

            273

            The basic objective of the Cripps Mission was to achieve the Indian support for ______?

            Correct Answer: World War II
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Cripps Mission (1942) was aimed at securing Indian support for the British war effort during World War II.

            The mission promised post-war self-government in exchange for support in the war, but it failed to satisfy Indian leaders.

            274

            Which mineral is found in Saindak (Balochistan)?

            Correct Answer: Copper
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Copper mineral is found in Saindak (Baluchistan)
            • **
            • Saindak project is operated with the collaboration of China
            • Project Location: Chaghi, Balochistan
            • **
            • The discovery of gold, copper & silver deposits at Saindak was made in the 1970s
            • Trial production at the Saindak Copper-Gold project started in 1995.
            275

            ماحول کو صاف ستھرا رکھنا ہمارا _____ فریضہ ہے؟

            Correct Answer: قومی
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            ماحول کی صفائی ہمارے قومی فریضے میں شامل ہے کیونکہ یہ تمام شہریوں کے لیے ذمہ داری ہے۔

            اس میں ہر فرد کا کردار اہم ہے تاکہ ہم اپنے ملک کو صاف ستھرا رکھ سکیں۔

            276

            Who is known as the founder of Tahrik-e-Raishmi Romal (The Silk Letter Movement)?

            Correct Answer: Maulana Mahmood ul Hassan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Maulana Mahmood ul Hassan was an Indian Muslim scholar and an independence activist

            The Silk Letter Movement, also known as Tehreek-e-Reshmi Rumal

            It was initiated by Deobandi leaders, particularly Maulana Mahmood Hasan Deobandi

            This movement, active between 1913 and 1920,

            Its headquarters were in Kabul,

            They aimed to achieve Indian independence from British rule by allying with the"

            • Ottoman Empire
            • Afghanistan
            • Germany

            The letters, written on silk cloth, were intercepted by the Punjab CID, revealing the plot. 

            277

            Quaid-e-Azam resigns/left from Indian National Congress in which year?

            Correct Answer: 1920
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In 1920, Mohamed Ali Jinnah resigned from Congress due to a congress campaign of Satyagraha

            Jinnah joined Indian National Congress in 1906.

            Mohamed Ali Jinnah became a member of the All India Muslim League on October 10, 1913.

            ***

            278

            Which is the smallest river of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Ravi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Ravi does not flow from Jammu and Kashmir.

            River Ravi originates from the state of India Himachal Pradesh.

            It flows along the Indo-Pak border before entering Pakistan.

            The Ravi River is the smallest river among five other rivers of the Indus basin system flowing in Pakistan.


            ***

            while

            24 rivers are present in Pakistan

            5 rivers flow in Punjab Province

            4 rivers flow in Sindh Province

            7 rivers flow in Balochistan Province

            8 rivers flow in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Province

            ND7-5-2023


            279

            Pakistan Steel Mills is located in?

            Correct Answer: Karachi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan Steel Mill is headquartered in Karachi, Sindh

            it was founded on  30 Dec 1973 

            it was Founded with the support of Russia.

            Zulfikar Ali Bhutto leads the foundation stone of Pakistan Steel Mills Corporation  on the 15th of January 1985


            280

            The shrine of Abdullah Shah Ghazi is located in?

            Correct Answer: Karachi
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • The Abdullah Shah Ghazi Mazar is located in Karachi.
            • Location: Shahrah-e-Firdousi, Block 4/Block 3, Clifton, Karachi.
            • It is a renowned 8th-century Sufi shrine 
            • originally built by Murshid Nadir Ali Shah of Sehwan Sharif
            • Born/: 98 Hijri Or 109 Hijri.
            • Died: 151 AH ≈ 773 (aged 52–53)CE
            • Known for: Sufi mysticism

            ****

            Last updated by NS on 01 MAY 2026

            281

            How many Pakistanis won Noble Prize So far?

            Correct Answer: 2
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            1. Dr. Abdul Salam (1979)

            2. Malala Yousafzai (2014)

            282

            Who divides powers in a government to prevent control by one party or person?

            Correct Answer: The Constitution
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             The Constitution is the document that outlines the structure and functions of a government, including:

            • The principle of separation of powers, which divides authority into different branches (legislative, executive, and judicial)
            • To prevent any one branch from becoming too powerful
            283

            When was Liaquat Nehru Pact between Pakistan and India?

            Correct Answer: 1950
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Liaquat Nehru pact was signed to deal with the issue of Rights of minorities in both countries.

            The treaty was signed in New Delhi by the Prime Minister of India Jawahar Lal Nehru and the Prime Minister of Pakistan Liaquat Ali Khan.

            Liaquat Nehru Pact was signed on April 8, 1950.
            284

            Where are the Chaukhandi graves located?

            Correct Answer: 29 km east of Karachi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Chaukhandi graves are located 29 km east of Karachi, Sindh province of Pakistan.

            They are famous for their delicate stone carvings with intricate geometric patterns.

            285

            Who won the 1965 presidential election in Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Ayub Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Date of election: March 21, 1965

            Winner: Ayub Khan

            Result: Ayub Khan won 62.43% of the Electoral vote

            Fatima Jinnah's vote share: 35.86%

            286

            The first Political Party that was formed in the history of the Indo-Pak was ______?

            Correct Answer: Congress
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Indian National Congress was founded on 28 December 1885 

            It was founded at Gokuldas Tejpal Sanskrit College in Bombay 

            Its Headquarters is located in New Delhi

            It was founded by Allan Octavian Hume.


            Additional information

            Allan Octavian Hume Place was appointed as the General Secretary,

            Womesh Chunder Bonnerjee of Calcutta was elected the first President.


            287

            After the failure of the war of independence in 1857, the British made the lives of __________ miserable.

            Correct Answer: Muslims
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Indian Rebellion of 1857 fought from: 10 May 1857 to 1 November 1858

            288

            The Pirpur Report on the brutalities of the Congress Ministries (1937-39), was compiled by?

            Correct Answer: Raja Muhammad Mehdi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            On March 28, 1938, the Council of ML appointed an eight-member committee under the presidency of Raja Syed Muhammad Mehdi of Pirpur. 

            They presented their report on November 15, 1938.

            They tried to dig out the cruelties of the Congress ministries in seven provinces.


            289

            The first water accord was signed among the provinces in which year?

            Correct Answer: 1991
            Show explanation
            Explanation
          • The Water Apportionment Accord (WAA) was signed by provinces on 16 March 1991.
          • It was approved by the Council of Common Interests (CCI) on 21 March 1991.
          • Clause 13 of WAA recognized the need to establish the Indus River System Authority (IRSA).
          • IRSA was created to implement the Accord.
            • 290

            Quaid-e-Azam met M.K. Gandhi for the first time in 1916 at which city?

            Correct Answer: Lucknow
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Lucknow Pact is an agreement between the Indian National Congress (INC) and the All-India Muslim Federation.

            Signed in 1916 at a joint meeting of  the two parties in Lucknow.

            291

            Around how much percentage electricity is thermal?

            Correct Answer: 70%
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Around 70% of the world's electricity is generated from thermal power plants, which use coal, natural gas, or oil to generate electricity.

            This remains the dominant source of electricity worldwide despite the growing use of renewable energy.

            292

            Who laid down the foundations of the art of the novel in Sindhi language?

            Correct Answer: Mirza Qaleech Baig
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Mirza Qaleech Baig is considered the founder of the art of the novel in Sindhi literature.

            Zeenat was the first original novel in the Sindhi language.

            Zeenat was published in 1854.

            293

            Name the tactical ballistic missile launched by Pakistan in May 2025?

            Correct Answer: Abdali
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan conducted a successful training launch of the Abdali weapon system

            The missile has range of 450 kilometres (280 miles)

            The test of the Abdali Weapon System was conducted on May 3, 2025 as part of a military drill titled Exercise INDUS.

            This missile is also known as Hatf-II

            This missile was developed by SUPARCO

             

            Additional Information:

            SUPARCO stands for Pakistan's Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission.

            SUPARCO is the space agency of Pakistan.

            Abdali missile is named after Ahmad Shah Abdali.

            He was the 18th-century founder of modern Afghanistan.

             *****

             

            294

            Pakistan launched its ballistic missile on 3rd May 2025 with a range of 400 kilometers which is name after which of the following?

            Correct Answer: Ahmad Shah Abdali
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan conducted a successful training launch of the Abdali weapon system

            The missile has range of 450 kilometres (280 miles)

            The test of the Abdali Weapon System was conducted on May 3, 2025 as part of a military drill titled Exercise INDUS.

            This missile is also known as Hatf-II

            This missile was developed by SUPARCO

             

            Additional Information:

            SUPARCO stands for Pakistan's Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission.

            SUPARCO is the space agency of Pakistan.

            Abdali missile is named after Ahmad Shah Abdali.

            He was the 18th-century founder of modern Afghanistan.

             *****

             

            295

            What is the range of surface-to-surface missile named Abdali launched by Pakistan in May 2025 in miles?

            Correct Answer: 280 miles
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan conducted a successful training launch of the Abdali weapon system

            The missile has range of 450 kilometres (280 miles)

            The test of the Abdali Weapon System was conducted on May 3, 2025 as part of a military drill titled Exercise INDUS.

            This missile is also known as Hatf-II

            This missile was developed by SUPARCO

             

            Additional Information:

            SUPARCO stands for Pakistan's Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission.

            SUPARCO is the space agency of Pakistan.

            Abdali missile is named after Ahmad Shah Abdali.

            He was the 18th-century founder of modern Afghanistan.

             *****

             

            296

            What is the other name of Abdali weapon system launched by Pakistan in May 2025 with a range of 400 km?

            Correct Answer: Hatf-II
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan conducted a successful training launch of the Abdali weapon system

            The missile has range of 450 kilometres (280 miles)

            The test of the Abdali Weapon System was conducted on May 3, 2025 as part of a military drill titled Exercise INDUS.

            This missile is also known as Hatf-II

            This missile was developed by SUPARCO

             

            Additional Information:

            SUPARCO stands for Pakistan's Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission.

            SUPARCO is the space agency of Pakistan.

            Abdali missile is named after Ahmad Shah Abdali.

            He was the 18th-century founder of modern Afghanistan.

             *****

             

            297

            What is the range of Abdali weapon system launched by Pakistan in May 2025?

            Correct Answer: 400 kilometres
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Pakistan conducted a successful training launch of the Abdali weapon system

            The missile has range of 450 kilometres (280 miles)

            The test of the Abdali Weapon System was conducted on May 3, 2025 as part of a military drill titled Exercise INDUS.

            This missile is also known as Hatf-II

            This missile was developed by SUPARCO

             

            Additional Information:

            SUPARCO stands for Pakistan's Space and Upper Atmosphere Research Commission.

            SUPARCO is the space agency of Pakistan.

            Abdali missile is named after Ahmad Shah Abdali.

            He was the 18th-century founder of modern Afghanistan.

             *****

             

            298

            صلاح الدین ایوبی رح اپنے کارناموں میں کس پر بازی لے گئے تھے؟

            Correct Answer: نور الدین زنگی
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            صلاح الدین ایوبی نے نور الدین زنگی کے زیر قیادت مسلمانوں کے لئے عظیم کارنامے انجام دیے، خاص طور پر یروشلم کی بازیابی میں۔

            انہوں نے نور الدین کی رہنمائی کو آگے بڑھایا اور صلیبی جنگوں میں مسلمانوں کی فتوحات کو یقینی بنایا۔

            299

            Tipu Sultan was martyred during the war in ______?

            Correct Answer: Seringapatam Fort
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Tipu Sultan, also known as the Tiger of Mysore

            Fateh Ali was the real name of Tipu Sultan or Tiger of Mysore

            Tipu Sultan was the ruler of the Kingdom of Mysore based in South India

            Tipu Sultan  was Born: on December 1, 1751, in Devanahalli, India

            Tipu Sultan Died: on May 4, 1799, in Srirangapatna, India

            Tipu Sultan wrote a letter to France to help him against British Army rule in India. 

            Tipu Sultan was Buried: on May 5, 1799, Mausoleum of Tipu Sultan, Srirangapatna

            The third battle of Mysore is also known as Tipu Sultan`s battle against the British

            300

            In the province of ______ the provincial Muslim league formed a committee headed by Mr. Sharif to investigate the unjust proceeding of the congress.

            Correct Answer: Bihar
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Sharif Report (March 1939) was prepared by Mr. Sharif and his team to investigate injustices against Muslims under Hindu-dominated Congress rule.

            It focused mainly on the ill-treatment of Muslims in Bihar, documenting discrimination and oppression by the provincial government.

            301

            In which field the people of ancient Indus Valley Civilization and of the Gandhara Civilization were expert?

            Correct Answer: In the art of melting and casting bronze into various forms
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The people of the Indus Valley and Gandhara Civilizations were skilled in metallurgy, especially in casting bronze to create tools, statues, and ornaments.

            Notable examples include the famous "Dancing Girl" statue of Mohenjo-Daro, made of bronze using the lost-wax casting method.

            302

            Who took notice of the exploitation of the poor peasants at the hands of land owners and approved an act in 1859?

            Correct Answer: Lord Canning
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Lord Canning took notice of the exploitation of poor peasants by landowners in India.

            He approved the 1859 Tenancy Act, aimed at protecting the rights of tenants and regulating the relationship between landlords and peasants.

            303

            What is the total population of Pakistan lives in urban (cities) areas?

            Correct Answer: 36.33%
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            63.66% of Pakistan's population lives in rural areas.

            36.33% of the population resides in urban areas.

            304

            Wullar lake is the source of which river?

            Correct Answer: Jehlum
            Show explanation
            Explanation

             Wular Lake is the source of the Jhelum River.

            It is located in Jammu and Kashmir

            This lake also has a small island in its centre called the 'Zaina Lank'.

            305

            جب ملتان میں محمد بن قاسم کو بہت بڑا خزانہ ہاتھ لگا جس میں بے پناہ مال و دولت کے علاوہ سونا شامل تھا، تو اس نے دیبل کے بندر گاہ کے ذریعے کہاں روانہ کردیا؟

            Correct Answer: عراق
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            جب محمد بن قاسم کو ملتان میں بہت بڑا خزانہ ملا، جس میں سونا اور مال و دولت شامل تھا، تو اس نے یہ خزانہ عراق روانہ کیا۔
            خزانے کو عراق بھیجا گیا تاکہ وہ وہاں کے حکام کو دیا جائے اور خزانے کا صحیح استعمال کیا جائے۔
            306

            Which of the following Award was a gift for the Indian Minorities?

            Correct Answer: Radcliff Award
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Communal Award was introduced by the British Prime Minister Ramsay MacDonald in 1932 to provide separate electorates for various minority communities in India, including Muslims, Sikhs, and Dalits.

            It was aimed at addressing the demands of minorities and offering them political representation.

            307

            How many Muslim princes were hanged in November 1857 at Delhi?

            Correct Answer: 24
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            In November 1857, during the Indian Rebellion, 24 Muslim princes were hanged in Delhi as part of the British response to the uprising.

            Indian Rebellion of 1857 fought from: 10 May 1857 to 1 November 1858

            308

            بو علی سینا ایک نامور حکیم، عالم ______ تھے۔

            Correct Answer: حیاتیات و طبعیات
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            بو علی سینا ایک مشہور حکیم اور عالم تھے جنہوں نے حیاتیات (Biology) اور طبعیات (Physics) میں اہم کام کیا۔

            ان کی تصنیف "القانون فی الطب" طب کے میدان میں ایک عظیم کارنامہ ہے۔

            309

            Shah Jehan was son of King Jehangir. His real name was _____?

            Correct Answer: Khurram
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Shahab-ud-din Muhammad Khurram was the original name of Shah Jahan.

            He was the fifth Mughal emperor from 1628 to 1658.

            He was the son of Emperor Jehangir.

            He is famous for building the Taj Mahal.

            310

            The Indian National Congress Committee session was held on 14th June 1947, where in 3rd June Plan was accepted _____?

            Correct Answer: Unhappily
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The Indian National Congress accepted the 3rd June Plan unhappily, as it involved the partition of India.

            They agreed reluctantly, considering it the only practical solution to avoid further conflict.

            311

            Islamia College Peshawar was founded in ______?

            Correct Answer: 1913
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            • Islamia College is a public university located in Peshawar.
            • Founded by Abdul Qayoom Khan in 1913.
            • Quaid-e-Azam visited 3 times to Islamia College Peshawar in:
            • 1936
            • 1945 
            • 1948
            312

            ______ forests are found on the Delta of river Indus and river Hub; trees in these forests are of small size.

            Correct Answer: Coastal forests
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Coastal forests are found on the delta of the Indus and Hub rivers, especially in the form of mangroves.

            Trees in these forests are typically small in size and adapted to saline conditions.

            313

            Which of the following is the second largest industry of Pakistan?

            Correct Answer: Sugar industry
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            The sugar industry is the second largest agro-based industry in Pakistan after textiles.

            It plays a vital role in the rural economy by supporting millions of farmers and employing thousands in sugar mills.

            314

            According to the Nehru Report _________ should be made National Language.

            Correct Answer: Hindi
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            According to the Nehru Report (1928), Hindi was proposed to be made the national language of India.

            The report emphasized Hindi in the Devanagari script as a unifying language for the country.

            The Nehru Report of 28 August 1928 was a memorandum to appeal for new dominion status and a federal set-up of government for the constitution of India.


            315

            In January 1929, all parties Muslim conference met under the Presidentship of _______ at Delhi.

            Correct Answer: Agha Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation
            The All India Muslim Conference was held in Delhi between the dates of 31st December 1928 and 1st January 1929.
            The conference was held under the Presidentship of Sir Sultan Mohamed Shah (Aga Khan III) with Fazal Rahimtoola being its Secretary.
            316

            The British government did not oppose the Lahore Resolution because British were worried by the ______?

            Correct Answer: Second World War
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Second World War started: September 1939.

            Lahore Resolution presented: 23 March 1940.

            317

            Balochistan was given the status of province during the era of ______?

            Correct Answer: Yahya Khan
            Show explanation
            Explanation

            Balochistan got the status of a province: on 1 July 1970.

            Balochistan was given the status of a province during the era of Yahya Khan. 

            "Baluchistan" important question and information:

            Baluchistan covers 42 percent of the area of Pakistan

            Baluchistan province is located in South West of Pakistan.

              Baluchistan became a province in 1970.

                Capital of Baluchistan is Quetta.

                  Baluchistan is the biggest province of Pakistan by area.

                  Balochistan is located on the Iranian plateau.

                  Sajji dish belongs to Balochistan

                  35 districts are there in Balochistan

                  There are many rivers in Balochistan but 7 are the main

                  Hingol is the largest river in the Balochistan

                  Baluchistan province has 7 divisions.

                  318

                  Life insurance companies were brought under government influence in which year?

                  Correct Answer: 1972
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Zulfikar Ali Bhutto nationalised 32 insurance companies.

                  It later merged and consolidated them under SLIC in November 1972.

                  319

                  The five points of Prime Minister Muhammad Khan Junejo announced on ______?

                  Correct Answer: December 1985
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  President Zia-ul-Haq nominated Muhammad Khan Junejo as the Prime Minister of Pakistan on March 20, 1985.

                  Prime Minister Muhammad Khan Junejo announced his five-point reform program in December 1985.

                  The points focused on Islamization, economy, accountability, law and order, and administrative reforms.

                  320

                  Reference to Nehru report by all parties convention, Jinnah formulated the Muslim demands, which later on come to be known as his ______?

                  Correct Answer: 14 points
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  In response to the Nehru Report (1928), which ignored Muslim concerns, Jinnah presented his Fourteen Points in 1929 to safeguard Muslim rights in India.

                  These points became the foundation of the Muslim League's demands and a key step toward the Two-Nation Theory.

                  321

                  In 1919, the Khilafat Conference held its first meeting in the city ______?

                  Correct Answer: Delhi
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  Khilafat movement was launched in the subcontinent in 1919 till 1924.

                  The First Khilafat Conference was held in Delhi on 23 November 1919, to protest against the British policy towards the Ottoman Empire and to express support for the Caliphate.

                  322

                  When did Gandhi call off Non-cooperation Movement?

                  Correct Answer: February 1922
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                  in February 1922, Gandhi himself called off the movement.

                  A mob of people at Chauri – Chaura (near Gorakhpur) clashed with police and burnt 22 policemen on February 5, 1922.

                  This compelled Gandhiji to withdraw from the Non-Cooperation movement on Feb. 12, 1922. 

                  Gandhiji had to stop his Non-co-operation movement because the people became violent at Chauri-Chau.

                  Non-cooperation movement started in 1920.

                  323

                  What is the name of the father of "Shah Waliullah"?

                  Correct Answer: Shah Abdur Rahim
                  Show explanation
                  Explanation

                   Shah Abdur Rahim, the father of Shah Waliullah.

                  Real name of Shah Waliullah: Qutubuddin Ahmad Syed.
                  51 books were written by Shah Waliullah.
                  Shah Waliullah Dehlawi was Born in 21 February 1703 in Phulat, Mughal Empire.
                  Shah Waliullah Dehlawi Died in 20 August 1762 (aged 59) Dehli, Mughal Empire.
                  Shah Waliullah first time in history translated the Holy Quran into the Persian Language.
                  Shah Wali Ullah was born in the reign of Aurangzeb.

                  ****
