                    4th Martial Law

                    12th October 1999

                    General Pervaiz Musharraf

                     

                     

                    386

                    All Parties Conference was held in ______?

                    Correct Answer: 1928
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The All Parties Conference was a group of Indian political parties known for organizing a committee in May 1928 to author the Constitution of India after independence was actualized.

                    It was chaired by Dr. M. A. Ansari.

                    387

                    According to Labour Force Survey 2020-21, total labour force in Pakistan is ____?

                    Correct Answer: 71.76 million
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    According to the Labour Force Survey 2020-21, the total labour force in Pakistan was 71.76 million.

                    This figure represents the number of people who are either employed or actively seeking employment during that period.

                    388

                    The Shrine of Lal Shahbaz Qalander is situated in _____?

                    Correct Answer: Sehwan Sharif
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The shrine of Hazrat Laal Shahbaz Qalandar is situated in Sehwan Sindh

                    Lal Shahbaz Qalandar wrote several books in Persian and Arabic

                    Lal Shahbaz Qalandar was a Sufi saint and poet of present-day Pakistan and Afghanistan

                    The full name of Hazrat Lal Shahbaz Qalandar: Syed Shah Hussain Marwandi

                    Born: 1177, Marand, Iran

                    Died: February 19, 1274, Sehwān

                    Place of burial: Darbar Hazrat Usman Marvandi Lal Shahbaz Qalandar, Sehwān

                    Title: Qalandar

                    389

                    The Shimla Agreement was signed after which conflict?

                    Correct Answer: Indo-Pakistani War of 1971
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Shimla Agreement was signed after the Indo-Pakistani War of 1971.

                    It was signed on July 2, 1972, following the war and the subsequent creation of Bangladesh.

                    The agreement aimed to establish a path toward durable peace between India and Pakistan. 

                    390

                    Rajaji Formula was given in which year?

                    Correct Answer: 1942
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    It was a proposal formulated by Chakravarti Rajagopalachari

                    C. R. formula is also known as the Rajaji formula 

                    In 1942, Chakravarti Rajagopalachar opposed the Congress party by differing against the Quit India Movement 

                     

                    C. R.'s formula solves the political deadlock between the All India Muslim League and the Indian National Congress 


                    The CR formula acted as the basis for the negotiations. Gandhi and Jinnah met in September 1944 to ease the deadlock.

                    391

                    Pakistan won its first-ever FIFA World Cup qualifier match against which country?

                    Correct Answer: Cambodia
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    On 17 October 2023, Pakistan's men's football team secured their first-ever FIFA World Cup qualifying victory by defeating Cambodia 1–0 at the Jinnah Sports Stadium in Islamabad. 

                    The decisive goal was scored by Harun Hamid.

                    392

                    Who was the first Pakistani to climb Mount Everest?

                    Correct Answer: Nazir Sabir
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The first Pakistani to climb Mount Everest was Nazir Sabir.

                    He achieved this feat on May 17, 2000, as part of a team on the Mountain Madness Everest Expedition.

                    He was also the first Pakistani to climb K2, Gasherbrum II, Broad Peak, and Gasherbrum I (Hidden Peak).

                    393

                    The Ministry of Aviation in Pakistan was merged into which ministry?

                    Correct Answer: Ministry of Defence
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    In February 2025, the federal government of Pakistan merged the Ministry of Aviation into the Ministry of Defence as part of a rightsizing initiative aimed at reducing state expenditures and improving efficiency. 

                    This restructuring led to the abolition of the standalone Ministry of Aviation, with its functions and responsibilities now falling under the purview of the Ministry of Defence.

                    394

                    Who has become the first spinner to take 100 wickets in the history of Pakistan Super League (PSL)?

                    Correct Answer: Shadab Khan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Shadab Khan has become the first spinner to take 100 wickets in the Pakistan Super League during clash against Multan Sultans in the PSL 10.

                    The right-arm spinner reached the milestone by dismissing Multan Sultans’ captain Mohammad Rizwan.

                    He became the fourth player and first spinner to take 100 wickets in the history of the PSL.


                    Additional Information: 

                    Shadab Khan represents Islamabad United in PSL 2025.

                     ****

                    395

                    What is the installed electricity generation capacity of Pakistan as of March 2024?

                    Correct Answer: 42,131 MW
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    As of March 2024, Pakistan's total installed electricity generation capacity stood at 42,131 megawatts (MW). 

                    The energy mix comprises approximately 59.4% thermal, 25.4% hydel, 8.4% nuclear, and 6.8% renewable sources.

                    396

                    Who was the first female judge of a High Court in Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Justice Majida Rizvi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Justice Majida Rizvi became the first female judge of a High Court in Pakistan when she was appointed to the Sindh High Court in 1994.

                    She paved the way for women in Pakistan's judiciary and

                    She later served as the Chairperson of the National Commission on the Status of Women (NCSW).

                    397

                    HEC (Higher Education Commission) was established in ______?

                    Correct Answer: 2002
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    HEC was established in 2002, replacing the University Grants Commission (UGC).

                    Dr. Atta-ur-Rahman was the first chairman of HEC.

                    398

                    What was Pakistan's per capita income in fiscal year 2023-24?

                    Correct Answer: $1,680
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    According to the Economic Survey 2023-24, Pakistan's per capita income increased to $1,680, up from $1,551 the previous year. 

                    This 8.3% rise was attributed to improved economic activity and a stable exchange rate.

                    399

                    Who became first woman foreign minister in the history of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Hinna Rabbi Khar
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Hinna Rabbi Khar belongs: Muzaffargarh district (party PPP).

                    Hinna Rabbi Khar appointed first woman foreign minister: 2011 (during PPP government)

                    400

                    What is Pakistan's largest stock exchange?

                    Correct Answer: Karachi Stock Exchange
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Karachi Stock Exchange (now part of Pakistan Stock Exchange) was the largest and most active in the country.

                    It played a central role in Pakistan's capital market before merging into the unified Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) in 2016.

                    401

                    What is the endpoint of the CPEC (China-Pakistan Economic Corridor) route?

                    Correct Answer: Gwadar
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    CPEC aims to build highways, railways, and pipelines linking Gwadar to Kashgar (Xinjiang).

                    The total length of the corridor is approximately 2700 km.

                    It's a key part of China–Pakistan relations.

                    402

                    Desert of Thar lies in the province of ______?

                    Correct Answer: Sindh
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    About Thar Desert

                    15% located in Pakistan

                    85% located in India

                    Also called Friendly Desert

                    Located near Badin, Sindh 

                    Famous for: Sand 

                    Coal also found in Thar 

                    also known as Great Indian Desert

                    7th largest desert on the planet.
                    3rd largest in Asia.
                    Area: 77,000 Miles

                    ****************

                    403

                    How many Polling Stations were declared Sensitive by The Election Commission of Pakistan (ECP) out of 85,307?

                    Correct Answer: 20,789
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    In the 2018 general elections, the Election Commission of Pakistan (ECP) declared 20,789 polling stations as sensitive out of a total of 85,307 established across the country. ​


                    These sensitive polling stations were distributed across the provinces as follows:​

                    • Khyber Pakhtunkhwa: 7,386
                    • Sindh: 5,776
                    • Punjab: 5,686
                    • Balochistan: 1,768
                    • Islamabad Capital Territory: 173​
                    404

                    How many prisoners in jails were denied right to vote in the elections of 2018?

                    Correct Answer: 80K
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                     In the 2018 general elections in Pakistan, approximately 80,000 prisoners across the country were denied their right to vote.

                    405

                    Out of 106 million voters how many female voters were included in the electoral role to cast vote in general election 2018?

                    Correct Answer: 46.73 million
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Out of 106 million voters, 46.73 million female voters are included in the electoral roll to cast a vote in the general election 2018

                    406

                    ANP leader Haroon Bilour was contesting me election from __constituency?

                    Correct Answer: PK-78
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Haroon Bilour and his father were both candidates for the PK-78 constituency for the Khyber Pakhtunkhwa Assembly.

                     He was killed in a suicide bombing during a party meeting in Peshawar on 10 July 2018.
                    Political party: Awami National Party
                    407

                    The whole Government of India Act of 1935 came into operation in Provincial part?

                    Correct Answer: 3rd April 1937
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Government of India Act of 1935 came into effect in the provinces of India in 1937.

                    The act was passed by the British Parliament in 1935.

                    408

                    Afghanistan Pakistan Action Plan for Peace and Solidarity APAPPS has been initiated by?

                    Correct Answer: Afghanistan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    ​The APAPPS was a joint initiative launched by both Pakistan and Afghanistan in 2018. 

                    (APAPPS) stands for Afghanistan-Pakistan Action Plan for Peace and Solidarity 

                    The first Review session of the APAPPS was held in Islamabad on 10 June 2019.

                     It was designed to establish a structured framework for bilateral cooperation in areas such as security, counter-terrorism


                    409

                    Wafaqi Mohtasib (Federal Ombudsman) was established on _____?

                    Correct Answer: 23rd January 1983
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Office of Wafaqi Mohtasib was established on 24 January 1983 under President's Order No. 1 of 1983.

                    It was formed by President Zia-ul-Haq to address public complaints against government maladministration.

                    410

                    Article 50 of the Constitution of Pakistan is concerned with ______?

                    Correct Answer: Composition of the Parliament
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Article 50 defines the Parliament of Pakistan as consisting of the President, the National Assembly, and the Senate.

                    It falls under Part III of the Constitution, relating to the structure of the Federation.

                    411

                    Soan River flows near ______?

                    Correct Answer: Rawalpindi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Soan River flows through the Pothohar Plateau.

                    It is passes near Rawalpindi in Punjab, Pakistan.

                    It is an important tributary of the Indus River system and contributes to the region’s drainage.

                    412

                    Where is Kalabagh located?

                    Correct Answer: Mianwali
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Kalabagh is located in the Mianwali District of Punjab, Pakistan.

                    It is well-known for the proposed Kalabagh Dam project.

                    Kalabagh is famous for the mineral of Iron.

                    369 million tons iron deposit.

                    413

                    Pakistan’s Constituent Assembly was constituted on ______?

                    Correct Answer: July 20, 1947
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan’s Constituent Assembly was officially constituted on July 20, 1947, under the Indian Independence Act 1947. This assembly served dual roles:

                    Legislative: Acting as Pakistan’s first parliament.

                    Constitutional: Drafting the country’s first constitution.

                    First Session: Held on August 10–14, 1947, in Karachi.

                    President: Jogendra Nath Mandal (temporary chair) and later Muhammad Ali Jinnah as permanent President.

                    414

                    Who founded the Sikh Empire and the first independent Sikh State in the Indian subcontinent?

                    Correct Answer: Maharaja Ranjit Singh
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Maharaja Ranjit Singh founded the Sikh Empire in 1799 in the Indian subcontinent.

                    The empire lasted until 1849, when it was annexed by the British East India Company.

                    He was the founder of the first independent Sikh State.

                    The Sikh Empire was centered in Punjab and extended across northwestern India.

                    415

                    "The Bible, The Quran and Science" is written by?

                    Correct Answer: Maurice Bucaille
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                     The book by Dr. Maurice Bucaille, The Bible, the Quran, and Science,

                    In 1976, Bucaille published 

                    416

                    Which celebrity among the following contested General Elections 2018? Jawad Ahmad , Shehzad Roy , Bushra Ansari

                    Correct Answer: Jawad Ahmad
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                     Jawad Ahmed is a Pakistani pop singer and musician turned politician.

                    Jawad Ahmed is currently the chairman of the Barabri Party Pakistan, a leftist party.

                    In May 2017, Jawad launched his leftist Barabri political party.

                    The party fielded 14 candidates in the 2018 elections, 

                    417

                    The new Capital of Pakistan was named as "Islamabad" in February, 1960 by?

                    Correct Answer: General Ayub Khan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                     In February 1960, the President's Cabinet of Pakistan decided to name the new capital of Pakistan as "Islamabad". 

                    The idea for the city was conceived by President Ayub Khan

                    It was built to replace Karachi as the capital 

                    418

                    To encounter terrorism throughout the country, Pakistan Army launched Operation?

                    Correct Answer: Radd-ul-Fasaad
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Operation Radd-ul-Fasaad was a counter-terrorism operation launched by the Pakistan Army in February 2017

                    It is Part of the:

                    • Insurgency in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
                    • The Insurgency in Balochistan
                    • Sectarian violence in Pakistan,
                    • The War on Terror

                    Date: 23 February 2017 – 22 July 2024

                    Result

                    • Operation Azm-e-Istehkam continues
                    • 375,000 intelligence-based operations conducted as of 2021
                    • Terrorist group cells dismantled
                    419

                    The Black Hole episode occurred in the regime of _____?

                    Correct Answer: Shuja-ud-Daula
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Black Hole of Calcutta incident happened in 1756 when British prisoners were allegedly confined in a small room by the troops of Nawab Siraj-ud-Daula.

                    This event was one of the factors that led to the Battle of Plassey in 1757, where Siraj-ud-Daula was defeated by the British.

                    420

                    The head of Joint Investigation Team (JIT) to probe Panama was?

                    Correct Answer: Wajid Zia (FIA).
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    He was appointed as DG FIA by Prime Minister Imran Khan in November 2019.

                    He has previously served as Inspector General of Railway Police.

                    He first rose to prominence when he headed the joint investigation team to probe the Panama Papers case in 2017.

                    421

                    Dasht River is located in the province of ______?

                    Correct Answer: Baluchistan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    There are many rivers in Balochistan but 7 are the main

                    The important rivers in Balochistan are:

                    Zhob

                    Nari

                    Bolan

                    Pishin

                    Lora

                    Mula

                    Hub

                    Porali

                    Hingol is the largest river in the Balochistan

                    Rakshan

                    Dasht.

                    422

                    The largest Railway workshop Mughalpura is situated in _____?

                    Correct Answer: Lahore
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                     Moghalpura Railway Workshops are situated in Lahore, Pakistan. 
                     Railway Workshops were established in 1897 since the British Rule
                     Railway Workshops is the largest workshop in Pakistan
                    423

                    Who inaugurated the IDEAS 2018 (International Defence Exhibition and Seminar) at Karachi Expo Center on 27th November 2018?

                    Correct Answer: President Dr. Arif Alvi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The International Defence Exhibition and Seminar referred to as IDEAS, 

                    It was inaugurated by the President Dr. Arif Alvi

                    The event, a biennially organized mega-regional event, was held on November 27th, 2018. 


                    424

                    The Government of India Act, 1935 was enforced in _______?

                    Correct Answer: July 1935
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    The Government of India Act, 1935 was an Act adapted from the Parliament of the United Kingdom.
                    It originally received royal assent in August 1935.
                    It was the longest Act of (British) Parliament ever enacted until Greater London Authority Act 1999 surpassed it.
                    425

                    Which sector is the largest consumer of oil and oil production in Pakistan/World?

                    Correct Answer: Transport Sector
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    The transport sector is the largest consumer of oil and oil production.
                    The search for oil in Pakistan started in 1868.
                    426

                    The first nuclear power plant in Pakistan was set up at Karachi in ____?

                    Correct Answer: 1972
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    On 28 November 1972, President Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto inaugurated the Karachi Nuclear Plant 


                    **

                    Karachi Nuclear Power Plant (KANUPP) Unit 1. KANUPP is Pakistan’s first nuclear power plant, 

                     With a total gross capacity of 137 MW.


                    K2 & K3 with the help of China 

                    K2  started on 21 May 2021 and has a 1100 MW capacity.

                    K3 is expected to start in 2022.

                    **
                    ND13-1-2023


                    427

                    Which judge of the Lahore High Court was recently appointed to the Supreme Court of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Justice Baqar Najafi
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    On 15 April 2025, the Judicial Commission of Pakistan nominated Justice Ali Baqar Najafi of the Lahore High Court for elevation to the Supreme Court.

                    The decision was made by a majority vote of the Commission's total membership.

                    428

                    How many votes needed the Prime Minister of Pakistan to win the confidence vote?

                    Correct Answer: 168
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Total number of seats in the National Assembly of Pakistan: 336

                    votes needed the Prime Minister of Pakistan to win the confidence vote: 168

                    Under which article: Article 58
                    ****

                    429

                    Qasim Suri resigned as deputy speaker National Assembly of Pakistan on _________?

                    Correct Answer: April 16th 2022
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Qasim Suri became Deputy Speaker National Assembly: August 2018

                    Qasim Suri Belongs: PTI (Balochistan)

                    430

                    Who was the first federal law minister of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Jogendra Nath Mandal
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Jogendra Nath Mandal was the first federal law minister of Pakistan.

                    He was appointed to the position in 1947.

                    He played a significant role in the early governance of the country.

                    431

                    Which is the biggest industry of Pakistan?

                    Correct Answer: Textile
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Cotton textile production and apparel manufacturing are Pakistan's largest industries.

                    It accounts for about 65% of the merchandise exports

                    The textile industry employs about 45% of the country's labor force. 

                    Pakistan is the fourth largest producer of cotton in the world. 

                    Pakistan is the eighth largest exporter of textiles in Asia. 

                    432

                    What was the voter turnout percentage in Pakistan's general elections held in February 2024?

                    Correct Answer: 47.6%
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    According to the Free and Fair Election Network (FAFEN), the voter turnout in the February 8, 2024 general elections was 47.6%, a decrease from 52.1% in 2018.

                    Despite the percentage drop, the absolute number of voters increased, with approximately 60.6 million people casting their votes, compared to 54.8 million in 2018.

                    The decline in turnout percentage is attributed to a significant increase in the number of registered voters, which rose from 106 million in 2018 to 128.6 million in 2024.

                    433

                    When did Chaudhry Fazal Elahi resign from the office of President?

                    Correct Answer: 16 September 1978
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Chaudhry Fazal Elahi resigned from the presidency on 16 September 1978.

                    He was the first President of Pakistan after the 1973 Constitution was enacted.

                    434

                    The area of ex-Federally Administratered Tribal Areas (FATA) is _______ square kilo meters.

                    Correct Answer: 27220 km
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation
                    • The area of ex-Federally Administrated Tribal Areas (FATA) is 27220 square kilometers.
                    • ***
                    • The Federally Administered Tribal Areas (FATA) consisted of two types of areas:
                    • 1.      Tribal Agencies (Tribal Districts)
                    • 2.      Frontier Regions (FRs).
                    • There were seven Tribal Agencies and six Frontier Regions
                      • ****

                    Related Question to FATA:

                      • Share of FATA in NFC award 3%
                      • Operation Zarb-e-Azb underwent in the former FATA. Azb was the name of the Sword of the Holy Prophet (PBUH)
                      • Name of Army operation in FATA? Zarb-e-Azb
                      • The major languages of FATA are Pashto
                      • kPK seats before FATA merger _124
                      • General seats in the KPK assembly after the merger of FATA are 145
                      • FATA was merged with KPK under which constitutional amendment 25th
                      • Ex-FATA comprises 7 Agencies









                    435

                    What was the total debt of Pakistan in the year 2024?

                    Correct Answer: Rs. 74.013 Trillion
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    As of June 2024, Pakistan’s total public and guaranteed debt reached approximately Rs. 74.013 trillion.

                    This figure represents a significant rise, mainly due to currency devaluation and fiscal deficits.

                    436

                    In June 2018, Pakistan alongside _________________ has shown interest in becoming member of the International Solar Alliance (ISA).

                    Correct Answer: China
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    In June 2018, Pakistan, alongside China, expressed interest in joining the International Solar Alliance (ISA). 



                    437

                    Pakistani named among powerful people in the world 2018 by Forbes Magazine is?

                    Correct Answer: General Qamar Bajwa
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Qamar Javed Bajwa NI(M), HI(M)

                    It is a retired Pakistani four-star general 

                    He served as the tenth chief of the Army Staff of Pakistan from 29 November 2016 to 29 November 2022.

                    In 2018, he was ranked 68th in the Forbes list of the World's Most Powerful People.

                    438

                    Who wrote to England, "My present policy is based on mercy without any weakness; it will not affect the government supremacy"?

                    Correct Answer: Lord Canning
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Lord Canning was the first Viceroy of India after the Indian Rebellion of 1857.

                    He was serving from 1858 to 1862.

                    His policy of "mercy without weakness" aimed at restoring stability and maintaining British control over India after the rebellion.

                    439

                    Which country offered its military and police services to the UN for its peace keeping missions in various conflict zones all over the world?

                    Correct Answer: Pakistan
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Pakistan has a long history of contributing to United Nations peacekeeping missions, providing both military and police personnel to various conflict zones worldwide.

                    The Pakistan Armed Forces are the third largest contributor of troops towards UN peacekeeping efforts, behind India and Ethiopia.

                    440

                    In 1997, Pakistan and Iran, joined by _________ other developing countries and formed the D-8.

                    Correct Answer: Six
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    D-8 is the economic group of Nine Muslim countries.

                    The D-8 is an organization of nine Developed countries:

                    Short trick to learn D-8 BETii - MNP

                    1. Bangladesh
                    2. Egypt
                    3. Indonesia
                    4. Iran
                    5. Malaysia
                    6. Nigeria
                    7. Pakistan
                    8. Turkey
                    9. Azerbaijan

                    India is not a member of the D-8 

                    **

                    The D-8 Organization for Economic Cooperation, also known as Developing-8

                    The D-8 Organization for Economic Cooperation Headquarters: İstanbul, Türkiye

                    D8 was Established in Istanbul in 1997.

                    The Founder of D-8 Organization for Economic Cooperation: Necmettin Erbakan

                    441

                    Mahmood Ghaznavi attacked Raja Jaipal of Lahore in 1001 A.D. Battles were fought at ______ and Hund (Swabi).

                    Correct Answer: Peshawar
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    Battle of Peshawar was fought between Mahmud of Ghazni and the Hindu Shahi army of Jayapala, near Peshawar.

                    Mahmood Ghazni is known as the “Abductor of Scholars”.

                    17th attack of Sultan Mehmood Ghazni on India was took place in 1027 AD.

                    442

                    At the time of Partition, how many industries were in British India, of which only 34 came under Pakistan’s jurisdiction?

                    Correct Answer: 921
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    At the time of Partition in 1947, British India had 921 industries in total.

                    Out of these, only 34 industries were located in the territories that became Pakistan, showing the economic disparity Pakistan faced initially.

                    443

                    Which agreement signed between India and Pakistan in the year 1960?

                    Correct Answer: Indus Water Treaty
                    Show explanation
                    Explanation

                    The Indus Waters Treaty (IWT) is a water-distribution treaty between India and Pakistan.

                    Indus Waters Treaty brokered by the World Bank

                    It was signed in Karachi on 19 September 1960

                    Sighed by:

                    • Indian Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru 
                    •  Pakistani President Ayub Khan.

                    The Treaty allocates the 3 Western Rivers to Pakistan

                    1.  Indus
                    2. Jhelum
                    3. Chenab  

                    the 3 Eastern Rivers  to India

                    1. Sutlej
                    2. Beas
                    3. Ravi

                      *************

                      444

                      Free trade policy was adopted during ________ period.

                      Correct Answer: Lord Lytton
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Lord Lytton served as Viceroy of India from 1876 to 1880.

                      His administration introduced the Free Trade Policy in 1879, removing restrictions on imported goods to boost British industrial exports to India.

                      This policy favored British manufacturers at the expense of Indian industries.

                      445

                      Who enforced the Basic Democracies System in 1959 to introduce a new Political System in the country?

                      Correct Answer: Ayub Khan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      EBDO was promulgated by Ayub Khan in 1959. 

                      EBDO stand for Electoral bodies dissolution order.

                      446

                      General Zia-ul-Haq ruled Pakistan from ______?

                      Correct Answer: 1977 to 1988
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      All about Zia-ul-Haq

                      Zia-Ul-Haq Died on 17-08-1988 by Plane crashed in Bahawalpur

                      American Ambassador Arnold Raphael with President Zia Ul Haque in a plane crash

                      General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq HI, GCSJ, ร.ม.ภ, was a Pakistani four-star general 

                      Muhammad Zia-Ul-Haq became the CMLA of Pakistan in: 1977

                      Born: August 12, 1924, Jalandhar, India

                      Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq took oath as President of Pakistan on 23 March 1985.

                      Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq becomes 6th President of Pakistan.

                      General Zia Ul Haq ruled Pakistan from 1977 to 1988

                      The provincial constitution order martial law in 1977.

                      When PCO was introduced by Zia ul Haq? 1981

                      When General Zia Ul Haq addressed in UN? 23 Oct 1985

                      He remains the country's longest-serving de facto head of state and Chief of Army Staff

                      Ghulam Ishaq Khan succeeded Zia Ul Haque as President of Pakistan.

                      In the Era of Zia ul Haq who was the Prime Minister of Pakistan: Muhammad Khan Junejo.

                      447

                      According to the Fourteen Points, residuary power should be vested ______?

                      Correct Answer: In the Provinces
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      According to Quaid-e-Azam Muhammad Ali Jinnah's Fourteen Points (1929), the residuary powers should be vested in the provinces.

                      He ensuring that the provinces had authority over matters not specifically enumerated in the constitution.

                      This was part of Jinnah's demand for safeguarding the rights of the Muslim-majority provinces in British India.

                      448

                      The General election of 1945-46 was held in ______?

                      Correct Answer: 11 Provinces
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The General Election of 1945-46 was held in 11 provinces of British India.

                      It included the provinces under the British rule, excluding princely states.

                      Lord Wavell was the Viceroy of India at the time.

                      449

                      کس کے والد کو انگریزوں نے موت کی سزادی؟

                      Correct Answer: محمد حسین آزاد
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      محمد حسین آزاد کے والد، مولوی محمد باقر کو 1857 کی جنگِ آزادی میں انگریزوں نے موت کی سزا دی۔

                      وہ پہلے صحافی تھے جنہیں آزادیِ اظہار کی پاداش میں شہید کیا گیا۔

                      450

                      The Hindu society divided into four different castes, with regard to their ______?

                      Correct Answer: Professions
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      1. Brahmins – Priests, scholars, and teachers (highest caste).
                      2. Kshatriyas – Warriors and rulers.
                      3. Vaishyas – Traders and merchants.
                      4. Shudras – Laborers and service providers.
                      451

                      _____ most important deed was his imposition of a refined tax system.

                      Correct Answer: Akbar's
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Akbar introduced an efficient and fair tax system called the Zabt system based on land measurement.

                      It increased revenue collection and reduced corruption, becoming a model for future administrations.

                      452

                      An important event in the reign of Muhammad Shah was the attack of ______ on India.

                      Correct Answer: Nadar Shah
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Nadar Shah of Persia invaded India in 1739 during Muhammad Shah's reign.

                      He defeated the Mughals at the Battle of Karnal and looted Delhi, taking the Peacock Throne and Koh-i-Noor diamond.

                      453

                      The reign of which Mughal Emperor is said to be the "Golden Era"?

                      Correct Answer: Shah Jahan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Shah Jehan became emperor in 1628.

                      The reign of Shah Jahan is considered the "Golden Era" of the Mughal Empire.

                      It was marked by architectural achievements like the Taj Mahal, and the empire reached its cultural and economic peak.

                      454

                      The biggest road constructed by Sher Shah Suri reaches from Peshawar to ______ is known as Grand Trunk (GT) Road.

                      Correct Answer: Bengal
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      GT RoadDetail
                      Full Form Grand Trunk Road
                      Constructed  bySher Shah Suri
                      Sher Shah Suri Real nameFareed Khan
                      455

                      Babar ruled India for how many years?

                      Correct Answer: 4
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      • Babar ruled India for 4 years.
                      • He established the Mughal Empire in 1526.
                      • He reigned until his death in 1530.
                      • Babar was the first Mughal king to rule India.
                      456

                      Indus Valley Civilization is also known as _______?

                      Correct Answer: Harrapan Civilization
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Indus River Valley Civilization, 3300-1300 BCE.

                      It is also known as the Harappan Civilization.

                      It extended from modern-day northeast Afghanistan to Pakistan and northwest India.

                      457

                      What was Sir Shah Nawaz Bhutto's role in the princely state of Junagadh in 1947?

                      Correct Answer: Dewan (Prime Minister) D)
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Sir Shah Nawaz Bhutto served as the Dewan (Prime Minister) of Junagadh in 1947

                      He was a member of the Bhutto family, hailing from Larkana 

                      He was born on 8 March 1888 in Garhi Khuda Bakhsh

                      Died: 19 November 1957 (aged 69), Larkana

                      Resting place: Bhutto family mausoleum

                      458

                      Pakistan’s renowned TV, Radio host, film actor, and politician Tariq Aziz passed away on which date?

                      Correct Answer: 17 June 2020
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Tariq Aziz, Pakistan’s renowned TV and radio host, film actor, and politician, passed away on 17 June 2020 in Lahore at the age of 84.

                      He was best known for hosting PTV’s iconic quiz show Nilaam Ghar (The Tariq Aziz Show and Bazm-e-Tariq Aziz)

                      He was also the first person to appear on Pakistan Television (PTV) when it launched in 1964.​

                      459

                      Governor General ______ prepared a plan for the division of India and approved it from the British Government.

                      Correct Answer: Lord Mountbatten
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Lord Mountbatten, the last British Governor General of India, prepared and presented the Mountbatten Plan in 1947.

                      This plan led to the partition of India and the creation of Pakistan.

                      460

                      When were the citizens of FATA given the right of vote?

                      Correct Answer: 1997
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The citizens of FATA (Federally Administered Tribal Areas) were granted the right to vote in 1997 general elections.

                      Before this, only Maliks (tribal elders) had voting rights under a limited franchise system.

                      461

                      _________ belt of Afghanistan separate Tajikistan from Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Wakhan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The Wakhan Corridor is a narrow strip of territory in northeastern Afghanistan that extends to China. 

                      It separates Tajikistan from Pakistan.

                      It is lies between Pakistan’s Gilgit-Baltistan region and China’s Xinjiang province.



                      462

                      Deoband was a small town of _____?

                      Correct Answer: Saharanpur
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      About Deoband Madrasah

                      Founded: 31 May 1866

                      Founder: Muhammad Qasim Nanautavi

                      First Principal: Maulana Muhammad Qasim

                      Location: Saharanpur, Uttar Pardesh

                      463

                      What was the cause of Sheikh Rehman’s death?

                      Correct Answer: Assassinated
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Sheikh Mujibur Rahman was a Bangladeshi politician

                      Assassinated: August 15, 1975, Dhaka, Bangladesh

                      He served as Bangladesh's first President and Prime Minister from April 1971 to 15 August 1975.

                      Party: Awami League

                      He is referred to as the "Father of the Nation" in Bangladesh. 

                      Sheikh Mujib was arrested during Ayub Khan's era.

                      He was a key figure in the movement against Ayub Khan's military rule.

                      Born: March 17, 1920, Tungipara, Bangladesh

                      464

                      Agra fort constructed by ______?

                      Correct Answer: Akbar
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Akbar made it his capital and arrived in Agra in 1558.

                      His historian, Abul Fazl, recorded that this was a brick fort known as 'Badalgarh'.

                      It was in a ruined condition and Akbar had it rebuilt with red sandstone from Barauli area Dhaulpur district, in Rajasthan.

                      465

                      The social code of conduct of Pashtuns is called?

                      Correct Answer: Pashtunwali
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                       Pashtunwali is the traditional lifestyle and code of the Pashtuns.

                      Pashtunwali is also known as Pakhtunwali and Afghaniyat

                      466

                      When was the Human Rights Commission of Pakistan established?

                      Correct Answer: 1986
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      HRCP was established in 1986 to promote human rights in Pakistan.

                      Registered in 1987

                      467

                      Which provincial assembly passed 10 economic zones?

                      Correct Answer: Punjab
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                       Punjab has established 10 Special Economic Zones. 

                      The 10 SEZs in Punjab include

                      1. JW China-Pakistan SEZ,
                      2. Allama Iqbal Industrial City,
                      3. Rahim Yar Khan Industrial Estate,
                      4. Bhalwal Industrial Estate,
                      5. Vehari Industrial Estate,
                      6. Rachna Industrial Park
                      7. M3 Industrial City
                      8. Value Addition City
                      9. RYK Industrial Estate
                      10. Quaid-e-Azam Business Park
                      468

                      Pakistan supported China on the Tiananmen Square issue in?

                      Correct Answer: 1989
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      In 1986, Pakistani president Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq visited China to improve diplomatic relations

                      Pakistan offered crucial support to China in the aftermath of the 1989 Tiananmen Square protests.


                      Additional information 

                      Tiananmen Square is a city square in the city center of Beijing, China,

                      They named it after the eponymous Tiananmen.

                      The founding of the People's Republic of China in the square was on October 1, 1949

                      The size of Tiananmen Square is 765 x 282 meters (215,730 m2 or 53.31 acres).

                      469

                      Colonel Sher Khan died in which war?

                      Correct Answer: Kargil War
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Karnal Sher Khan was martyred on 5 July 1999 during Kargil War between India & Pakistan.

                      Karnal Sher Khan was one of only eleven recipients of the Nishan-e-Haider.

                      He was a captain in the 27th Sindh Regiment and later was posted to 12th NLI Regiment during the Kargil Conflict.

                      He was killed in action during the Kargil war 1999.

                      470

                      What was Qazi Faez Isa’s tenure as Balochistan Chief Justice?

                      Correct Answer: 2009–2014
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                       Qazi Faez Isa served as the Chief Justice of the Balochistan High Court from 2009 to 2014


                      Qazi Faez Isa (29th CJP of Pakistan)

                      Born: October 26, 1959 (Quetta)

                      Became SC judge: September 5, 2014

                      Sworn in as CJP: 17th September 2023

                      Predecessor:  Umar Ata Bandial

                      Retirement: 25 October 2024

                      471

                      Share of Pakistan in cotton industries during partition was?

                      Correct Answer: 16
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                       At the time of the 1947 partition, Pakistan inherited a very small portion of the cotton industries in the subcontinent.

                      Specifically, it inherited only 16 out of the 921 industrial units that existed, and these mainly focused on cotton textiles.

                      472

                      پاکستان کی لمبائی تقریباً کتنے ہے؟

                      Correct Answer: 1600 kilometers
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      پاکستان کی لمبائی تقریباً 1600 کلومیٹر ہے۔

                      یہ لمبائی ملک کی شمالی سرحد سے جنوبی سرحد تک پھیلی ہوئی ہے۔

                      473

                      ہندوستان میں 1945-46ء کے انتخابات کا اعلان کس نے کیا؟

                      Correct Answer: لارڈ ویول
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      ہندوستان میں 1945-46ء کے انتخابات کا اعلان لارڈ ویول نے کیا تھا۔
                      یہ انتخابات برطانوی راج کے تحت ہندوستان میں متوقع آئینی اصلاحات اور مسلمانوں کی سیاسی نمائندگی کے حوالے سے اہم تھے۔
                      474

                      سن 1906 ء میں مسلمانوں کے حقوق کے تحفظ کے لیے قائم ہونے والی جماعت کونسی تھی؟

                      Correct Answer: آل انڈیا مسلم لیگ
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      سن 1906ء میں مسلمانوں کے حقوق کے تحفظ کے لیے آل انڈیا مسلم لیگ قائم ہوئی۔

                      اس جماعت کا مقصد مسلمانوں کے سیاسی حقوق کو محفوظ بنانا اور ان کے مفادات کی نمائندگی کرنا تھا۔

                      475

                      مسلمان رہنماؤں نے 1957ء کی جنگ آزادی کی ناکامی کے بعد کس طرح مسلمانوں کی رہنمائی کرنے کی کوشش کی؟

                      Correct Answer: تعلیمی ادارے قائم کرنے کے ذریعے
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation
                      سن 1857ء کی جنگ آزادی کی ناکامی کے بعد مسلمان پسماندہ ہو گئے۔
                      سر سید احمد خان جیسے رہنماؤں نے تعلیمی ادارے قائم کر کے مسلمانوں کی فکری اور تعلیمی رہنمائی کی۔
                      476

                      سر سید احمد خان کا دو قومی نظریہ بعد میں کس تحریک کی بنیاد بنا؟

                      Correct Answer: تحریک پاکستان
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      سر سید احمد خان کا دو قومی نظریہ مسلمانوں اور ہندوؤں کو الگ قومیں قرار دیتا ہے۔

                      یہی نظریہ تحریک پاکستان کی نظریاتی بنیاد بنا، جس کے نتیجے میں پاکستان وجود میں آیا۔

                      477

                      بنیادی سہولیات میں کون سی چیز شامل نہیں ہے؟

                      Correct Answer: فیکٹریاں
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      بنیادی سہولیات میں نکاسی آب، بجلی، اور صاف پانی شامل ہوتے ہیں۔

                      فیکٹریاں معاشی یا صنعتی ترقی کا حصہ ہیں، بنیادی سہولت نہیں۔

                      478

                      پاکستان کے جنوب میں کونسا سمندر واقع ہے؟

                      Correct Answer: بحیرہ عرب
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      پاکستان کے جنوب میں واقع سمندر بحیرہ عرب ہے۔

                      کراچی اور گوادر جیسے بندرگاہی شہر اسی سمندر کے کنارے واقع ہیں۔

                      479

                      پاکستان میں قدرتی گیس کس چیز کا حصہ ہے؟

                      Correct Answer: معدنی وسائل
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      قدرتی گیس ایک اہم معدنی وسیلہ ہے جو زمین کے اندر سے حاصل ہوتا ہے۔

                      پاکستان میں یہ توانائی کا بڑا ذریعہ ہے، خاص طور پر صنعتوں اور گھریلو استعمال کے لیے۔

                      480

                      پاکستان نے 1992 ء میں کس کی حکومت کو تسلیم کیا اور ان کے ساتھ سفارتی تعلقات قائم کیے؟

                      Correct Answer: افغان مجاہدین
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      پاکستان نے 1992ء میں کابل میں مجاہدین کی حکومت کو تسلیم کیا تھا۔

                      یہ اقدام افغانستان میں کمیونسٹ حکومت کے خاتمے کے بعد کیا گیا تھا۔

                      481

                      پاکستان دنیائے اسلام کے کس حصے میں واقع ہے؟

                      Correct Answer: وسط میں
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      پاکستان جغرافیائی طور پر اسلامی دنیا کے وسطی خطے میں واقع ہے۔

                      یہ ایشیا کے جنوب میں ہونے کے باوجود مسلم ممالک کے درمیان مرکزی محلِ وقوع رکھتا ہے۔

                      482

                      Under the act of 1919, who controlled several ministers and their departments directly?

                      Correct Answer: Governor
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Under the Government of India Act 1919, the system of Dyarchy was introduced in provinces.

                      Governors controlled the "reserved subjects" and ministers controlled "transferred subjects" under the supervision of the Governor.

                      483

                      Which organization announced the establishment of the All India Muslim League?

                      Correct Answer: Muhammadan Educational Conference
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The All India Muslim League was established on December 30, 1906, during the annual session of the Muhammadan Educational Conference in Dhaka.

                      Key figures like Nawab Salimullah Khan of Dhaka and Aga Khan III played pivotal roles in its formation.

                      484

                      Himalayas is a ____________

                      Correct Answer: Mountain
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                       The Himalayas are the world's youngest, tallest, and most populated mountain system

                      They include the world's highest mountains, like Mount Everest,

                      485

                      In which journal did Sir Syed Ahmed Khan express his views about the 1857 uprising?

                      Correct Answer: Asbab-e-Baghawat-e-Hind
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Sir Syed Ahmed Khan expressed his views on the 1857 uprising in his famous work Asbab-e-Baghawat-e-Hind, where he analyzed the causes behind the rebellion.

                      In this journal, he argued that the revolt was a result of misunderstandings and mismanagement, emphasizing the need for social reforms and education for Muslims.

                      486

                      Where did Lord Mayo establish a college for the children of princely rulers?

                      Correct Answer: Ajmer
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Lord Mayo, the Viceroy of India from 1869 to 1872.

                      He established a college for the children of princely rulers in Ajmer.

                      This college was known as the Mayo College.

                      It's purpose was to provide education to the heirs of the princely states.

                      487

                      Agriculture tax is levied on how much land in Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: 12.5 acres
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      In Pakistan, agriculture tax is applicable on holdings exceeding 12.5 acres of land.

                      488

                      سلطان صلاح الدین ایوبی کا اصل نام کیا ہے؟

                      Correct Answer: یوسف
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      سلطان صلاح الدین ایوبی کا اصل نام یوسف تھا۔

                      ان کا مکمل نام یوسف بن ایوب تھا، اور وہ صلاح الدین ایوبی کے لقب سے مشہور ہوئے۔

                      انہوں نے 12ویں صدی میں صلیبی جنگوں میں بڑی کامیابیاں حاصل کیں اور یروشلم کو صلیبیوں سے آزاد کرایا۔

                      489

                      In which year did Changez Khan die?

                      Correct Answer: 1227
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Genghis Khan died in 1227.


                      He was the founder of the Mongol Empire.

                      He was the first Great Khan.

                      He launched various Mongol invasions and conquered a vast territory.

                      In 1206, Genghis Khan took over the Mongol empire.

                      In 1227, he died. No one has been able to found his tomb up till now.


                      490

                      What is the percentage of the country's national population engaged in agriculture?

                      Correct Answer: 65%
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Approximately 65% of Pakistan's national population is engaged in agriculture.

                      Agriculture remains a major sector contributing to the country's economy, employing a large portion of the workforce.

                      491

                      Who was appointed as the first Chief Commissioner of Punjab?

                      Correct Answer: Lord Lawrence
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Lord Lawrence was appointed as the first Chief Commissioner of Punjab in 1859.

                      He played a key role in the consolidation of British control over the region following the Second Anglo-Sikh War.

                      492

                      According to most geography sources, the country located to the west of Pakistan is ______?

                      Correct Answer: Afghanistan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Afghanistan lies directly to the west of Pakistan, sharing a long border known as the Durand Line.

                      India is to the east.

                      China is to the northeast of Pakistan.

                      493

                      When did Zulifqar Ali Bhutto came into power?

                      Correct Answer: 20 December 1971
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Zulfiqar Ali Bhutto became the President of Pakistan on 20 December 1971, after the resignation of General Yahya Khan.

                      He later became the Prime Minister in 1973 under the new constitution.

                      494

                      What country is located to the east of Afghanistan?

                      Correct Answer: Pakistan
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Pakistan lies to the east of Afghanistan, with a shared border known as the Durand Line.

                      It was established in 1893.

                      This border stretches approximately 2,670 kilometers (1,660 miles).

                      495

                      How many Pakistan climate in regions?

                      Correct Answer: 4
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Pakistan's climate is generally characterized by four distinct climatic zones:

                      1. The highland zone,
                      2. The lowland zone,
                      3. Desert areas,
                      4. Coastal climate
                      496

                      Which leader announced the 3 June Plan, which divided British India into India and Pakistan?

                      Correct Answer: Lord Mountbatten
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      The 3rd June Plan, also known as the Mountbatten Plan, was proposed by Lord Mountbatten

                      On 3rd June, 1947, Viceroy Lord Mountbatten formulated a scheme 

                      The 3rd June Plan announced the partition of British India into 2 states:

                      • India
                      • Pakistan


                      This Plan was also known as the or Partition Plan 

                      It was the last plan for independence.

                      497

                      In which year Syed Jamal uddin Afhani died?

                      Correct Answer: 1897
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Syed Jamal Uddin Afghani was a Thinker.

                      Syed Jamal-u-din Afghani Born: 1838, Asadabad, Afghanistan.

                      Syed Jamal-u-din Afghani Died: March 9, 1897, İstanbul, Turkey.

                      Syed Jamal-u-din Afghani Cause of death: Cancer of the jaw.
                      498

                      Dr. Ruth Pfau was a physician from which country?

                      Correct Answer: Germany
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Dr. Ruth Pfau

                      • Date of Birth: 9 September 1929
                      • Date of Death: 10 August 2017
                      • Other Name: Mother Teresa of Pakistan (due to her work for leprosy patients)

                      Major Awards:

                      • Hilal-e-Imtiaz (Pakistan)
                      • Hilal-e-Pakistan
                      • Nishan-e-Quaid-i-Azam
                      • Staufer Medal (Germany)
                      • Dame of the Order of St. Gregory the Great (by Pope)
                      499

                      Quaid-e-Azam represented Muslim demands effectively in which important event?

                      Correct Answer: Lucknow Pact
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      In 1916, Quaid-e-Azam played a key role in the Lucknow Pact, where he united Hindus and Muslims on common political demands.

                      He effectively represented Muslim rights, gaining recognition for separate electorates and other constitutional safeguards.

                      500

                      In which year did the Simla Deputation (Muslim Delegation) meet the British Viceroy Lord Minto?

                      Correct Answer: 1906
                      Show explanation
                      Explanation

                      Led by the Aga Khan III at the Viceregal Lodge

                      The Shimla Deputation took place on 1st October, 1906.

                      Here, Lord Minto met with a deputation of 35 Muslim leaders led by Aga Khan.


                      The deputation aimed to convince Lord Minto, to advocate for greater political representation for Muslims in British India

                      Nice work getting this far. Consistency beats intensity — come back tomorrow and keep stacking sets.
